all-hands-ai/OpenHands#10483.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

# Documentation Issue: Windows WSL Setup Missing Ubuntu Installation Steps

## Problem
The Windows setup documentation `docs/usage/local-setup.mdx` instructs users to "run inside the WSL terminal" but doesn't explain how to install a Linux distribution (Ubuntu) that provides this terminal.

Current Windows prerequisites only mention:
1. Install WSL
2. Install Docker Desktop
3. "The docker command below to start the app must be run inside the WSL terminal"

## Issue
`wsl --install` often only installs the WSL framework, not Ubuntu. Users end up with no Linux distribution and cannot access a "WSL terminal" to run the required Docker commands.

## Proposed Fix
Add Ubuntu installation to Windows prerequisites section:

**Current:**
```
**WSL**
1. Install WSL.
2. Run `wsl --version` in powershell and confirm `Default Version: 2`.
```

**Proposed:**
```
**WSL**
1. Install WSL.
2. Run `wsl --version` in powershell and confirm `Default Version: 2`.

**Ubuntu (Linux Distribution)**
1. Install Ubuntu: `wsl --install -d Ubuntu` in PowerShell as Administrator.
2. Restart computer when prompted.
3. Open Ubuntu from Start menu to complete setup.
4. Verify installation: `wsl --list` should show Ubuntu.
```

## Additional Context
- Affects majority of Windows users attempting local setup
- `wsl` command may open docker-desktop distribution instead of Ubuntu
- Users should use `wsl -d Ubuntu` or search "Ubuntu" in Start menu to access correct terminal

---

Please follow the style and structure of the current documentation to fit naturally and logically.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#10489.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

# 🐞 [Bug]: [API] GET /api/conversations/{id} with long id returns 500 (expected 4xx JSON)
---
### Checklist
- [x] I searched existing issues and discussions.
- [x] I can reproduce on the latest local build.
- [x] Not related to model/provider credentials or quotas.

## 📜 Description
Requests with a very long {id} to GET /api/conversations/{id} return 500 instead of a 4xx JSON error.  
Expected: reject oversized IDs early and return 400/414 with a JSON error body.


---

## 📋 Environment & Setup

| Component            | Detail                                                                 |
|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 🚀 Deployment        | Docker (CLI)                                                            |
| 🔐 Auth              | Not required locally                                                    |
| 💻 macOS Version     | macOS Sequoia 15.4.1                                                    |
| 🐍 Python Version    | 3.12.x                                                                  |
| 🐳 Docker Version    | Docker Desktop 4.43.2 (199162)                                          |
| 📦 OpenHands Image   | `docker.all-hands.dev/all-hands-ai/openhands:0.53`                      |
| 📎 OpenHands Digest  | `docker.all-hands.dev/all-hands-ai/openhands@sha256:61b05c5d247d12f7b490d945f63216e25f7cbda1a5232671a09cc7bf3226ce92` |
| 🔧 Runtime Image     | `docker.all-hands.dev/all-hands-ai/runtime:0.53-nikolaik`               |
| 🤖 Model             | `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528:free`                             |


---

## 🧪 Steps to Reproduce

### Endpoint
`GET /api/conversations/{id}`

### STEP 1
```bash
# Generate a 5,000-character 'a' string
LONG=$(python3 -c "print('a'*5000)")
```
### STEP 2
```bash
# Hit the /conversations/ endpoint
curl -i "${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:3000}/api/conversations/$LONG"
```


---

## 🧯 Observed Behavior

- `500 Internal Server Error`
```
curl -i "${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:3000}/api/conversations/$LONG"
HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
date: Mon, 18 Aug 2025 22:34:17 GMT
server: uvicorn
content-length: 21
content-type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

Internal Server Error
```


---

## ✅ Expected Behavior
- A safe **4xx** response (e.g., `414 URI Too Long` or `400 Bad Request`).
- **JSON** error body only (no HTML).

---

MacOS

Or even better, we should start a `get_default_openhands_config_for_eval` function and replace the current `OpenHandsConfig` definition across ALL `run_infer.py` **Additional context** ### If you find this feature request or enhancement useful, make sure to add a 👍 to the issue

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#10732.body:
**What problem or use case are you trying to solve?**

The provider services has grown into a 1,000+ line monolith that mixed unrelated responsibilities (HTTP/GraphQL plumbing, repositories, branches, PRs, resolver/comment utilities, and microagent features). This made the code hard to test, extend, and reason about; encouraged duplication across providers; and complicated future evolution (e.g., GitHub Enterprise tweaks or feature-specific overrides).

With plans to bring SAAS code to the open, making these services more modular will help dedup the code and set us up for the future. The goal is to execute a refactor in incremental steps to end up with better looking codebase, and more test coverage.


**Describe the UX or technical implementation you have in mind**

```mermaid
graph TD
  S[GitHubService facade] --> R[ReposClient]
  S --> B[BranchesClient]
  S --> P[PRsClient]
  S --> V[ReviewsClient]
  S --> M[MicroagentsClient]
  R --> A[GitHubAPI]
  B --> A
  P --> A
  V --> A
  M --> A
  A --> H[httpx.AsyncClient]
  H --> G[GitHub REST]
  H --> Q[GitHub GraphQL]
  A --> T[TokenProvider]
```

A provider service should look like the following. The clients should implement their own protocols and interfaces. By opting for a composition we get

- Strong boundaries, simpler unit tests (mock GitHubAPI), clearer ownership of cross-cutting concerns (timeouts, retries, headers, pagination).
- Easier to evolve (e.g., add caching, instrumentation) in one place.


**Additional context**
This issue has been created for two reasons

- To track the progress of our refactor. At the moment, the service classes have been refactor into multiple mixims that are inherited to create the facade of the `GitHubService`. This was an intermediate step so we could make incremental changes towards having a composition based architecture

- To track the linked PRs for this refactor so that OpenHands can easily browse context on the work/discussions done so far



### If you find this feature request or enhancement useful, make sure to add a 👍 to the issue
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#10732.title: Refactor: Make integration services composition of clients

We can also write a unit test that makes sure that prices in the md file matches the prices in the json file.

### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues. ~~at least my self-hosted agent tell me so. ~~

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

**Sorry for the terrible title** but I think this is a real issue that causes unnecessary retries during installation.

Below are my console logs, which show my frustration as I tried everything, but still kept getting "timeout" errors.

https://pastebin.ubuntu.com/p/mQKkRrGYxh/

My internet connection isn't slow as I think, my download speed is usually 15-20 MB/s—yet I still can't download the image within 5 minutes. If a timeout occurs, everything is terminated, and the next attempt starts from scratch. 

I had to manually install the image and then use uvx to start it. Since this is the recommended default installation method, I think there should be more consideration for users in less-than-ideal environments and be more rubust. At the very least, there should be a asking whether the user wants to wait longer if the process can't finish within the given timeout.

I'm also curious: this recommended installation method was [introduced two months ago](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/commit/5e85986f3229402b13d3a03142626b6c36a97f2b), but has no one else encountered this issue? (Or is it just me that really needs to buy Starlink?)

latest

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11256.body:
## Describe the bug and reproduction steps

Weaker LLMs crash the agent in confirmation mode.

**Two main issues observed:**
1. LLM does not provide `security_risk` parameter
2. LLM fills parameters with wrong format (e.g., `view_range`)


## OpenHands Installation
CLI

## OpenHands Version
V1 branch

## Model Name
DeepSeek (various models)

## Operating System
MacOS

## Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

```
Error starting agent chat: Missing required parameters for function 'str_replace_editor': {'security_risk'}
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/openhands_cli/simple_main.py", line 30, in main
    run_cli_entry()
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/openhands_cli/agent_chat.py", line 142, in run_cli_entry
    runner.process_message(message)
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/openhands_cli/runner.py", line 75, in process_message
    self._run_without_confirmation()
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/openhands_cli/runner.py", line 79, in _run_without_confirmation
    self.conversation.run()
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/openhands/sdk/conversation/impl/local_conversation.py", line 236, in run
    self.agent.step(self._state, on_event=self._on_event)
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/openhands/sdk/agent/agent.py", line 206, in step
    raise e
  File "/Users/yangxiao/git/OpenHands_local/openhands-cli/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/openhands/sdk/agent/agent.py", line 180, in step
    response = self.llm.completion(
  ...
openhands.sdk.llm.exceptions.FunctionCallValidationError: Missing required parameters for function 'str_replace_editor': {'security_risk'}
```

## Proposed Fix

Currently toggling confirmation mode changes the confirmation policy, but doesn't actually remove the security analyzer itself. 

The fix should involve removing the security analyzer from the agent preset, and better error handling around this error with an explainable user message to disable confirmation mode. 

Tradeoff will involve invalidating the cache (due to system prompt changes), but should not be very expensive unless repeatedly toggling confirmation mode in a single conversation.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11256.title: CLI(V1): DeepSeek LLM fails tool calling due to missing security_risk parameter in V1 SDK

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11378.body:
## Summary
The OpenHands CLI crashes with a `FunctionCallValidationError` when the LLM generates an `execute_bash` function call without the required `security_risk` parameter.

## Error Details
```
Error: Missing required parameters for function 'execute_bash': {'security_risk'}
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/fn_call_converter.py", line 739, in _extract_and_validate_params
openhands.sdk.llm.exceptions.FunctionCallValidationError: Missing required parameters for function 'execute_bash': {'security_risk'}
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. **Enable security-based confirmation mode**:
   ```
   > Auto-confirm LOW/MEDIUM risk, ask for HIGH risk
   ```

2. **Trigger an execute_bash function call**:
   - Use any prompt that would cause the agent to run a bash command
   - The LLM generates a function call for `execute_bash`

3. **Observe the crash**:
   - Function call validation fails in `fn_call_converter.py`
   - CLI crashes with `FunctionCallValidationError`

## Expected Behavior
- The `execute_bash` function call should include all required parameters
- Function call validation should pass
- The command should execute normally

## Actual Behavior
- LLM generates `execute_bash` call without `security_risk` parameter
- Function call validation fails
- CLI crashes with unhandled exception

## Environment
- **OpenHands CLI version**: Latest (main branch)
- **Confirmation mode**: Auto-confirm LOW/MEDIUM risk, ask for HIGH risk
- **Agent-SDK integration**: Using new agent-sdk structure

## Technical Details

### Error Location
- **File**: `openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/fn_call_converter.py`
- **Line**: 739
- **Function**: `_extract_and_validate_params`

### Complete Stack Trace
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "openhands_cli/simple_main.py", line 45, in main
  File "openhands_cli/agent_chat.py", line 174, in run_cli_entry
  File "openhands_cli/runner.py", line 83, in process_message
  File "openhands_cli/runner.py", line 103, in _run_with_confirmation
  File "openhands/sdk/conversation/impl/local_conversation.py", line 245, in run
  File "openhands/sdk/agent/agent.py", line 209, in step
  File "openhands/sdk/agent/agent.py", line 187, in step
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/llm.py", line 476, in completion
  File "tenacity/__init__.py", line 338, in wrapped_f
  File "tenacity/__init__.py", line 477, in __call__
  File "tenacity/__init__.py", line 378, in iter
  File "tenacity/__init__.py", line 400, in <lambda>
  File "concurrent/futures/_base.py", line 449, in result
  File "concurrent/futures/_base.py", line 401, in __get_result
  File "tenacity/__init__.py", line 480, in __call__
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/llm.py", line 461, in _one_attempt
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/non_native_fc.py", line 82, in post_response_prompt_mock
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/fn_call_converter.py", line 957, in convert_non_fncall_messages_to_fncall_messages
  File "openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/fn_call_converter.py", line 739, in _extract_and_validate_params
openhands.sdk.llm.exceptions.FunctionCallValidationError: Missing required parameters for function 'execute_bash': {'security_risk'}
```

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue occurs in the function call validation process:

1. **LLM generates function call**: Creates `execute_bash` call without `security_risk` parameter
2. **Function call converter processes it**: `convert_non_fncall_messages_to_fncall_messages` in `fn_call_converter.py`
3. **Parameter validation fails**: `_extract_and_validate_params` expects `security_risk` parameter but doesn't find it
4. **Exception thrown**: `FunctionCallValidationError` crashes the CLI

## Impact
- CLI becomes completely unusable when this occurs
- User cannot proceed with their task
- No graceful error handling or recovery

## Suggested Solution

1. **Investigate why LLM omits `security_risk` parameter**:
   - Check if function definition includes `security_risk` as required
   - Verify prompt templates include security risk guidance

2. **Add better error handling**:
   - Catch `FunctionCallValidationError` and provide user-friendly message
   - Allow user to retry or modify the request

3. **Parameter defaulting**:
   - Consider defaulting missing `security_risk` parameter to `UNKNOWN` or `MEDIUM`
   - Log warning when parameters are auto-defaulted

## Files to investigate
- `openhands/sdk/llm/mixins/fn_call_converter.py` (validation logic)
- Function definition for `execute_bash` tool
- Prompt templates that define function calling format
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11378.title: CLI crashes with FunctionCallValidationError: Missing required parameter 'security_risk' for execute_bash

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11424.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

We are seeing lots of these errors.
`Failure to load repo "XXX/.openhands with all available providers."` (where XXX is the org name)

These are happening because we are checking for the `.openhands` (or on gitlab, `openhands-config`) repo to download it. Typically if we try to download a repo and it doesn't exist this is an error, but only in the case where we are downloading these config directories, it is expected that these directories may not exist. So only in this case, we should print an error at level "debug" saying "Org-level configuration not found at XXX/.openhands" (or openhands-config).

### OpenHands Installation

app.all-hands.dev

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

_No response_

None

CONTENT: Explore and analyze all accessible data files in the /workspace/datasets directory to:

1. Exploration: List the /workspace/datasets directory (if exists) in a non-recursive manner and capture the output.
2. Data Identification: Based on the exploration, identify data files (CSV, JSON, XLSX, sqlite, zip, gz, sx, or TXT) and file codecs (UTF-8, GBK, etc.) that are likely to contain experimental data. 
3. Data Analysis: Write an analysis script in /workspace/data_analyze (e.g., in Python) that:
    - Reads the identified data files.
    - Identify the data structure and determines how to read them.
    - For each files in /workspace/datasets
      - If it is a standlone tabular files: print the first 10 rows and random 10 rows ; 
      - If it is a database connection configuration file: connect and list all tables, identify required tables for the research question, then print the first 10 rows and random 10 rows of required tables; 
      - If it is a document file: search for related infomation about how to load required data, print the matched content;
      - decompress the file start the analysis again if it is a compressed file; 
      - copy the file content as is to "data_show.md" if it is a document file;
    - Saves the results in "data_show.md".
4. Final Checking:
    - Make sure that the data files are accessible and readable.
    - Make sure no errors occur during the analysis.
    - Make sure that the results does support the following data analysis.

IMPORTANT: You should ONLY interact with the tool provided to you AND NEVER ASK FOR HUMAN HELP. Make sure all scripts, tools, and data files are saved in /workspace/data_analyze. Do not train models, perform experiments, or generate LaTeX files in this task. You will have separate tasks for those steps.

Research Question:
What is the prediction precision of AKI in ICU patients when dynamically predicting each day based on the past two days of historical data?

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11552.body:
## Description

OpenHands automatically sets `reasoning_effort='high'` by default for all non-Gemini models, which causes 400 errors when using OpenAI models that utilize the Responses API (like `gpt-5-pro`, `gpt-5-codex`, `o3-pro`, etc.) with unverified organizations.

**OpenAI's official recommendations:**
- OpenAI recommends `reasoning_effort="medium"` as the default
- `reasoning.summary` should be opt-in, not automatically added
- Source: [OpenAI Reasoning Best Practices](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning-best-practices)

## Details

The issue was introduced/maintained in **PR OpenHands/OpenHands#9925** (commit `a32a6230`), which was intended to optimize Gemini 2.5 Pro performance by ~2.4x.


```python
# added in model_post_init (lines 174-180):
if self.reasoning_effort is None and 'gemini-2.5-pro' not in self.model:
    self.reasoning_effort = 'high'  # ← Still forces 'high' for non-Gemini!
```

**Intent:** Allow Gemini to use `reasoning_effort=None` which gets converted to `thinking={budget_tokens: 128}` for performance optimization.

**But:** All other models still receive `reasoning_effort='high'` by default.

## User Flow 

1. User initializes OpenHands with a Responses API model (e.g., `gpt-5-codex`)
2. `LLMConfig.model_post_init()` sets `reasoning_effort='high'` by default
3. LiteLLM detects model has `mode: "responses"` in its model cost map
4. LiteLLM activates Responses API bridge
5. Bridge automatically converts `reasoning_effort='high'` to `Reasoning(effort='high', summary='detailed')`
6. OpenAI rejects the request because `reasoning.summary` requires verified organization
7. Gets 400 error


## Related

I try this in mini-swe-agent and works (by default):
```yaml
model:
  model_name: "gpt-5-codex"
  # No reasoning_effort configured → Works! ✓
```
**This confirms the issue is with sending `reasoning_effort` to Responses API models, not with OpenHands specifically.**


## Proposed Solution

### Option 1: Remove Default Entirely (Recommended)

Remove lines 174-180 from `openhands/core/config/llm_config.py`:

```python
# Set reasoning_effort to 'high' by default for non-Gemini models
# Gemini models use optimized thinking budget when reasoning_effort is None
logger.debug(
    f'Setting reasoning_effort for model {self.model} with reasoning_effort {self.reasoning_effort}'
)
if self.reasoning_effort is None and 'gemini-2.5-pro' not in self.model:
    self.reasoning_effort = 'high'
```

- ✅ Aligns with OpenAI's recommendation that `reasoning.summary` should be opt-in
- ✅ Prevents errors for users with unverified organizations
- ✅ Avoids unexpected costs (reasoning tokens are expensive)
- ✅ Gemini optimization still works (keeps `None`, converts to `thinking` budget)
- ✅ Users can explicitly configure via config.toml when needed

### Option 2: Exclude Responses API Models

If a default is desired, exclude models known to use Responses API:

```python
RESPONSES_API_MODELS = [
    'gpt-5-pro', 'gpt-5-codex',
    'o3-pro', 'o3-deep-research',
    'o4-mini-deep'
]

if (self.reasoning_effort is None and
    'gemini-2.5-pro' not in self.model and
    not any(m in self.model for m in RESPONSES_API_MODELS)):
    self.reasoning_effort = 'medium'  # Use OpenAI's recommended default
    logger.debug(f'Set reasoning_effort to "medium" for {self.model}')
```

### Option 3: Add Configuration Flag

Let users explicitly enable reasoning effort defaults:

```python
enable_default_reasoning_effort: bool = Field(
    default=False,
    description='Enable default reasoning_effort for supported models'
)

if (self.enable_default_reasoning_effort and
    self.reasoning_effort is None and
    'gemini-2.5-pro' not in self.model):
    self.reasoning_effort = 'medium'
```

## Recommendation

**Option 1** is strongly recommended because:
- **Follows OpenAI's official guidance**: Reasoning features should be opt-in
- **Maintains Gemini optimization**: Gemini still gets `None` → `thinking` budget
- **Solve the Issue**: No more 400 errors for unverified organizations
- **Reduces costs**: No unexpected reasoning token charges
- **User control**: Users can still configure explicitly when needed


## User Configuration

Users CAN already configure `reasoning_effort` in their `config.toml`:

```toml
[llm]
model = "gpt-5-codex"
reasoning_effort = "medium"  # or "low", "high", "none"
# Or omit to keep as None (recommended for Responses API models)
```

**However:**
- Most users don't know they need to do this
- The default behavior forces `reasoning_effort='high'` if they don't configure it
- This causes 400 errors for unverified organizations using Responses API models
- CLI is packaged as a standalone binary/executable, so users cannot modify the source code (`llm_config.py`) to change this, they would need to rebuild it

## Related Issues

- OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#987 - Original issue report about this problem
- PR OpenHands/OpenHands#9925 (commit `a32a6230`) - Where the current behavior was introduced/maintained
- Related to LiteLLM's automatic `reasoning.summary` addition 


### OpenHands Installation

CLI

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

None

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

_No response_

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11599.body:
**What problem or use case are you trying to solve?**

The _is_stuck_action_observation_pattern method in stuck.py is critical for loop detection. It contains complex boolean logic (e.g., in the actions_equal variable) that is not fully covered by the existing test suite. The current test (test_is_stuck_repeating_action_observation_pattern) only validates the "happy path" where a loop is successfully detected.

This lack of coverage means we cannot be fully confident that the detector won't miss a loop (false negative) or incorrectly report a loop (false positive) in various edge cases.

**Describe the UX or technical implementation you have in mind**

I propose enhancing the unit tests for this method in tests/unit/controller/test_stuck.py by applying the Modified Condition/Decision Coverage (MC/DC) methodology.

The technical implementation will involve:

    - Analyzing the key decisions, specifically D1 (the length check) and D2 (the actions_equal logic).

    - Creating the necessary N+1 test cases required to prove the independence of each logical condition.

    - Implementing these new tests to validate all the failure scenarios (e.g., where the pattern is broken in different ways), not just the success scenario.

**Additional context**

This change will significantly increase the robustness of the StuckDetector and give us higher confidence in its behavior. I am currently working on this analysis and implementation and plan to submit a PR soon.


### If you find this feature request or enhancement useful, make sure to add a 👍 to the issue
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11599.title: Improve test coverage for _is_stuck_action_observation_pattern with MC/DC

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11801.body:
I've been noticing that OpenHands generates a lot of zombie processes in its container. It looks like this got reported here back in August 2025:
https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/issues/10189

And then the OP submitted a PR for the issue here (also in August 2025):
https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/10190

The PR did not get reviewed and merged and was eventually closed. I strongly encourage the team to look into this issue because it causes the agent to eventually get stuck. So far, I've had to completely restart the runtime when this happens.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11853.body: ## Description Currently, when observations are displayed in the UI as collapsible blocks, the command that generated the observation is not shown. This makes it difficult for users to understand what action led to a particular observation. ## Proposed Solution Display the command that was executed alongside the observation in the observation block in the UI. ## Example When a command like `ls -la` is executed and generates an observation, the observation block should show: - The command that was run (`ls -la`) - The output/result of that command ## Context This enhancement would improve the user experience by providing better context about what actions the agent is taking. ## Additional Notes Mentioned by users in discussion for better UI transparency.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11853.title: Display command in observation block in UI

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11857.body: ### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead). - [x] I have checked the existing issues. ### Describe the bug and reproduction steps V0: V1: **Root cause:** 1. V0 includes secret names in the system prompt via RuntimeInfo.custom_secrets_descriptions in additional_info.j2. 2. V1 uses AgentContext with system_message_suffix, but secret names are not included. 3. SecretRegistry.find_secrets_in_text() only matches when the exact secret name appears in the command. **Solution:** 1. Include secret names in system message suffix (recommended) - Add secret names (not values) to the V1 system_message_suffix when building the agent context. - Location: LiveStatusAppConversationService._build_start_conversation_request_for_user() 2. Extract secret names from the secrets dict passed to StartConversationRequest. 3. Format them in system_message_suffix (e.g., "Available secrets: MY_PUGS_NAME, API_KEY, ..."). 4. Include instructions that when a user asks about a secret, the agent should use echo $SECRET_NAME. ### OpenHands Installation Docker command in README ### OpenHands Version _No response_ ### Model Name _No response_ ### Operating System None ### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context _No response_

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11910.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

### Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
In a long‑running OpenHands conversation, after the conversation had been condensed/summarized a few times, the agent **re‑executed an old instruction that had been fully completed 
days earlier**, without me issuing that instruction again.                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                      
From the user’s perspective, this looks like the agent suddenly “deciding” to re‑run a prior task on its own. This is dangerous because a condensed conversation can cause            
**previous, already‑satisfied instructions to be treated as fresh commands**.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Environment                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- OpenHands install: from GitHub                                                                                                                                                      
  - Repo: `https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands.git`                                                                                                                             
  - Commit: `cd3e845872d1aa64f0c2ff5902ff24d7f90d1efd` (`git rev-parse HEAD`)                                                                                                         
- Host OS: Linux (paths like `/home/tim/OpenHands`, `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material`)                                                                                                
- OpenHands is being used with:                                                                                                                                                       
  - A local filesystem workspace containing D&D reference material at `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/`                                                                              
  - Long, multi‑day conversations                                                                                                                                                     
  - Conversation condensation enabled (the UI has explicitly said the thread was “condensed” a few times)                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
I don’t know the exact active config file on the server side, but the repo’s `config.template.toml` shows:                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- `agent.enable_history_truncation = true`                                                                                                                                            
- A `[condenser]` section with `type = "noop"` by default, but in practice my conversation was clearly being condensed/summarized (the UI informed me several times that the thread   
was condensed), suggesting a non‑noop condenser (e.g., `llm`, `amortized`, etc.) or default condenser behavior was in effect.                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Scenario / What I Was Doing                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                      
I have a long‑running project:                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- Building a D&D DM rules index from a local corpus at `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/`                                                                                             
- Files include `dm_main_index.json`, `dm_index_summary.txt`, `D&D Topic List.txt`, PDFs, DOCX files, etc.                                                                            
- I’m also using a Jetson Orin Nano as a local LLM backend, with Ollama models (`qwen2.5:3b`, `llama3.2:3b`), but that’s mostly orthogonal to this bug.                               
                                                                                                                                                                                      
At some point **days ago** in this same conversation, I gave the agent this instruction (paraphrased, but very close to exact text):                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                      
> See the index here: `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/`. Using that index, find and tell me how to do a perception skill check. Don't use any other sources besides what is in that index.                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
The agent **correctly complied at that time**, by:                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- Reading `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/dm_main_index.json`                                                                                                                        
- Using `grep`/`sed` to find entries about:                                                                                                                                           
  - “Wisdom (Perception) check”                                                                                                                                                       
  - “Passive Perception = 10 + Wisdom (Perception) check modifier”                                                                                                                    
  - D20 Tests and ability checks from the DMG and SRD excerpts                                                                                                                        
- Explaining how to run a Perception skill check based solely on those index snippets.                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
That was all fine and expected.                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                      
After that, the conversation continued for days with **other topics**:                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- Installing/fixing Ollama models on the Jetson                                                                                                                                       
- Permissions for `/mnt/8tb_nvme/ollama_models`                                                                                                                                       
- Discussing the new D&D index pipeline, task tracking, etc.                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                      
The thread was **condensed** / summarized a few times in the UI (I saw explicit condensation messages) as it became long.                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### The Bug: Old Instruction Re‑Executed Without Being Given Again                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                      
Much later—**without me re‑typing that Perception/index instruction**—the agent suddenly:                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
1. Started running commands like:                                                                                                                                                     
   - `grep -ni "ability check" /home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/dm_main_index.json`                                                                                                    
   - `sed -n '10496,10515p' /home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/dm_main_index.json`                                                                                                       
   - `grep -ni "Perception" /home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/dm_main_index.json`                                                                                                       
   - `sed -n '58344,58364p' /home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/dm_main_index.json`                                                                                                       
   - etc.                                                                                                                                                                             
2. Then produced a detailed explanation of **how to do a Perception skill check**, referencing:                                                                                       
   - SRD and PHB snippets like:                                                                                                                                                       
     - “make a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check…”                                                                                                                                      
     - “Passive Perception = 10 + Wisdom (Perception) check modifier”                                                                                                                 
   - DMG guidance on when to call for a D20 Test and how to set DCs.                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                      
This behavior is **exactly what I had asked for days earlier** and which had already been completed. But at this later point:                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- I **had not just issued** that instruction.                                                                                                                                         
- We were in the middle of discussing something else (Jetson/Ollama, task tracking, etc.).                                                                                            
- The only reason the agent gave for doing this was that it saw the Perception instruction “at the top of the conversation” and treated it as fresh.                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                      
From my perspective as the user, it looked like the agent spontaneously decided to go back and re‑do an old task from days ago, without any corresponding new user request.           
                                                                                                                                                                                      
When I challenged it, the agent initially believed it had just responded to a user message that *it* could see in the current context:                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
> “See the index here: `/home/tim/DM_Reference_Material/`. Using that index, find and tell me how to do a perception skill check. Don't use any other sources besides what is in that 
index.”                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                      
However, I had **not** sent that message in the current turn‑sequence. That instruction was from **many days prior**, from an earlier portion of the same thread that had been        
condensed.                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                      
My conclusion is:                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- The condensation / summarization process **re‑introduced** that old instruction into the current context as if it were a recent user turn, and                                      
- The agent, seeing it as a current user input, obediently executed it again.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Why This Is Dangerous                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
This isn’t just cosmetic or a harmless repeat:                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- In my case, it was a safe action (reading index files and explaining rules), but the same pattern could apply to:                                                                   
  - Re‑running a destructive maintenance step.                                                                                                                                        
  - Re‑issuing a deployment, data deletion, or refactor command.                                                                                                                      
  - Repeating a one‑time migration.                                                                                                                                                   
- The user’s mental model is: **“Once an instruction is completed, it won’t be re‑run unless I explicitly ask.”**                                                                     
- The current condensation behavior appears to violate that assumption by:                                                                                                            
  - Surfacing a previously executed instruction as an active user message again, with no indication that it is historical or already handled.                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
This is especially concerning for long‑running “project” threads where instructions may have powerful side effects.                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Expected Behavior                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- Conversation condensation / summarization **must not** cause old user instructions to be replayed as if they were new, actionable commands.                                         
- Summaries should preserve **intent** and **state** (e.g., “Task X was already completed”), but should not re‑insert completed directives in a way that the agent misinterprets them 
as fresh instructions.                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Actual Behavior                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- After condensation, the system presented the agent with an old user instruction (the Perception/index query) as if it were current.                                                 
- The agent acted on it immediately, re‑running all the index searches and explanations.                                                                                              
- The user had not re‑issued this command in the recent turns.                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Hypothesis About Root Cause                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                      
I obviously don’t see the internals, but based on the conversation:                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
- The summarization / condensation layer appears to:                                                                                                                                  
  - Take earlier parts of the conversation and compress them.                                                                                                                         
  - Then reconstruct context for the agent by mixing:                                                                                                                                 
    - Some verbatim user turns.                                                                                                                                                       
    - Some summarized text.                                                                                                                                                           
- In the process, one of my **old user instructions** seems to have been:                                                                                                             
  - Re‑emitted as a direct, literal user message (“See the index… Using that index, find and tell me how to do a perception skill check…”),                                           
  - Without any marker that it is *historical* rather than a fresh input.                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
That is enough to cause the agent to treat it as a live directive.                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                      
---                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                      
### Suggested Constraints / Fix Direction                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
Some desirable properties for the condenser + planner:                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                      
1. **No resurrection of directives as fresh commands**                                                                                                                                
   When compressing history, do not emit historical user instructions as if they were new top‑level user messages. Historical directives should only appear:                          
   - In summarized narrative form (e.g., “Earlier, the user asked for X and the agent completed it”), or                                                                              
   - In a clearly demarcated “past context” that the agent can recognize as non‑actionable.                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                      
2. **Preserve completion state**                                                                                                                                                      
   The representation of history should encode that a given instruction was:                                                                                                          
   - Received at time T₀, and                                                                                                                                                         
   - Fulfilled at T₀+Δ,                                                                                                                                                               
   so the agent doesn’t “see” it as an unmet requirement.                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                      
3. **Optional: constraints on how condensers re‑inject user turns**                                                                                                                   
   If a condenser uses LLM summarization, it might be safer to:                                                                                                                       
   - Never regenerate old user messages verbatim in the “current” events list.                                                                                                        
   - Instead, keep old turns either:                                                                                                                                                  
     - Fully dropped, or                                                                                                                                                              
     - Summarized as past context explicitly labeled as such.                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                      
--- 

### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

None

_No response_

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11932.body:
## Problem

We currently maintain two parallel sets of `Settings` interfaces that represent the same data with different casing conventions:

https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/1a983d29781149187fb46a437da8e26dc02ac983/frontend/src/types/settings.ts#L40-L67

https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/1a983d29781149187fb46a437da8e26dc02ac983/frontend/src/settings-service/settings.types.ts#L3-L39

This requires **manual transformation** between the two in [`use-settings.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-settings.ts) and [`use-save-settings.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/use-save-settings.ts)

### Issues with the current approach

- **Maintenance burden**: Adding a new setting requires updating about 4 places (both type definitions + both transformation functions)
- **No type safety**: Nothing ensures the transformations stay in sync with the types
- **Error-prone**: Easy to miss a field or introduce bugs during refactoring
- **Unnecessary code**: ~60 lines of boilerplate transformation logic

## Proposed Solution

Consolidate to a single type using the `ApiSettings` snake_case format (the format returned directly from the API)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11932.title: Consolidate duplicate `Settings` type definitions

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11933.body:
## Problem

When a user hovers over their avatar in the sidebar, a context menu appears to the right of the avatar. To interact with the menu, the user must move their cursor from the avatar to the menu items. However, if the user moves their cursor diagonally or takes a direct path from the avatar to the menu, they may accidentally leave the hover zone, causing the menu to close unexpectedly. This is a classic "hover intent" UX problem where the hover zone doesn't account for the natural diagonal cursor path users take when moving between a trigger element and its associated menu. Current Behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f979366-5bd8-4b65-8be7-415dc4ffdacd

## Expected Behaviour

The user should be able to move their cursor from the avatar to the menu without the menu closing. The hover zone should include:

- The avatar itself
- The menu itself
- **The area above the avatar that runs parallel to the menu** (forming a safe "corridor" for diagonal cursor movement)

### Relevant files

[`user-actions.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/sidebar/user-actions.tsx)
[`account-settings-context-menu.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/context-menu/account-settings-context-menu.tsx)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11933.title: Avatar context menu closes when moving cursor diagonally to menu (hover zone gap)

The empty state should only appear when: - There is no error **AND** - The data has been successfully fetched **AND** - There are no conversations to display ### Related files [`recent-conversations.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/home/recent-conversations/recent-conversations.tsx#L67-L84)

## Problem

The `use-websocket.test.ts` tests are experiencing intermittent failures in CI while passing locally. Random tests within the file fail on different runs with no consistent pattern.

### Suspected cause

This may be related to the recent migration to Vitest v4 (#11887). Timing-sensitive WebSocket tests could be affected by changes in test isolation, async handling, or mock behavior in the new version.

### Current workaround

Re-run the CI jobs until they pass. The tests typically succeed within 1-3 attempts.

## Problem

The settings navigation items are currently used in two places with duplicated logic:

1. Account Settings Context Menu ([`account-settings-context-menu.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/context-menu/account-settings-context-menu.tsx#L27-L33)) - dropdown menu in the sidebar

Both locations perform the same logic:
- Check `APP_MODE` to determine whether to use `SAAS_NAV_ITEMS` or `OSS_NAV_ITEMS`

Create a `useSettingsNavItems()` hook that encapsulates this shared logic

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11951.body:
## Problem

Several test files are using `vi.mock` to mock Zustand stores, which is unnecessary given our test setup. Our custom Zustand mock in `__mocks__/zustand.ts` automatically resets stores between tests, and Zustand's API allows direct state manipulation via `store.setState()` and `store.getState()`.

Some files that use full store mocking with `vi.mock`:

`__tests__/components/browser.test.tsx` https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ed7adb335ca7e65f19418ca1f337f88c7b7f9c24/frontend/__tests__/components/browser.test.tsx#L33-L44

`__tests__/components/interactive-chat-box.test.tsx` https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ed7adb335ca7e65f19418ca1f337f88c7b7f9c24/frontend/__tests__/components/interactive-chat-box.test.tsx#L16-L19

`__tests__/components/chat/chat-interface.test.tsx` https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ed7adb335ca7e65f19418ca1f337f88c7b7f9c24/frontend/__tests__/components/chat/chat-interface.test.tsx#L30-L31

`__tests__/services/actions.test.tsx` https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ed7adb335ca7e65f19418ca1f337f88c7b7f9c24/frontend/__tests__/services/actions.test.tsx#L15-L21

### Why this is bad practice

1. Mock objects replicate store shape, which can drift from actual implementation
2. Doesn't test actual store behavior, reducers, or derived state
3. Requires manual reset in `afterEach` and recreating mock objects
4. Changes to store interface require updating both store and mock

## Preferred Patterns

**Pattern A: Direct state setting before render**

```ts
useBrowserStore.setState({ url: "https://example.com", screenshotSrc: "" });
renderWithProviders(<BrowserPanel />);
```

**Pattern B: Calling actions via `getState()` wrapped in `act()`**

```ts
act(() => {
  useCommandStore.getState().appendInput("echo hello");
});
```

**Pattern C: Using `renderHook` for testing store hooks directly**

```ts
const { result } = renderHook(() => useEventStore());
act(() => result.current.addEvent(mockEvent));
expect(result.current.events).toContain(mockEvent);
```

### Examples of Correct Usage

[`__tests__/stores/use-event-store.test.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/__tests__/stores/use-event-store.test.ts) - uses `renderHook` + `act()`
[`__tests__/components/terminal/terminal.test.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/__tests__/components/terminal/terminal.test.tsx) - uses `setState()` before render
[`__tests__/components/features/microagent-management/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/__tests__/components/features/microagent-management/microagent-management.test.tsx) - uses `setState()` with helper functions

## Acceptance Criteria

- Remove `vi.mock` for Zustand stores in the affected files
- Replace with `store.setState()` for initial state setup
- Use `act(() => store.getState().action())` for triggering actions
- Ensure tests still pass and maintain coverage
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11951.title: Refactor tests to use Zustand's native state setting instead of `vi.mock` for stores

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11952.body:
## Problem

The main MSW handlers file ([`frontend/src/mocks/handlers.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/mocks/handlers.ts)) still contains a bunch of lines of inline handlers that could be extracted into separate domain-specific files for better organization and maintainability. Several handler domains have already been successfully modularized:

- `billing-handlers.ts`
- `file-service-handlers.ts`
- `task-suggestions-handlers.ts`
- `secrets-handlers.ts`
- `git-repository-handlers.ts`

## Proposed Changes

Extract the remaining inline handlers into their own files

### Benefits

- Improved code organization and discoverability
- Easier maintenance of domain-specific mock logic
- Consistent architecture across all mock handlers
- Smaller, focused files that are easier to review and modify

## Acceptance Criteria

- All inline handlers extracted into domain-specific files
- `handlers.ts` only contains imports, aggregation, and shared exports
- All existing tests pass without modification
- `npm run dev:mock` and `npm run dev:mock:saas` work correctly
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11952.title: Refactor MSW `handlers.ts` into modular handler files

Test files are scattered across multiple `__tests__` folders within `frontend/src/` instead of being centralized in `frontend/__tests__/`. This inconsistency makes it harder to find and maintain tests.

## Current state

There are test files across 4 different `__tests__` folders inside `frontend/src/` _that I have found_, there may be more:
- [`frontend/src/utils/__tests__/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/utils/__tests__)
- [`frontend/src/components/features/settings/mcp-settings/__tests__/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/components/features/settings/mcp-settings/__tests__)
- [`frontend/src/services/__tests__/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/services/__tests__)
- [`frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/__tests__/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/__tests__)

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11991.body:
## Problem

In the task tracking UI that displays plan items in the chat, the "in progress" status shows a static loading icon instead of an animated spinner.

When a task has status: `”in_progress"`, the [`TaskItem`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/task-tracking/task-item.tsx) component renders a static SVG icon (`loading.svg`) that looks like a partial arc/circle. There is no animation applied, making it indistinguishable from a static icon at a glance.

## Expected Behaviour

The loading icon should animate (spin) to clearly indicate that the task is actively in progress, providing better visual feedback to users.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#11991.title: Task tracking loading indicator is static instead of animated spinner

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12014.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps


In `runtime=local`, the workspace and browser cache/download paths fall back to `/workspace` or a temp dir. On host machines `/workspace` is often missing or unwritable, leading to errors or files landing in a hidden temp. Playwright/BrowserGym can also throw `EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/workspace'` when using that default. We need local-friendly defaults while keeping Docker behavior unchanged.





Reproduction steps:

1) Set `RUNTIME=local` (no docker) with no `workspace_base`.
2) Start the server (e.g., INSTALL_DOCKER=0 RUNTIME=local make run / uvicorn …).
3) Workspace is `/workspace` or a temp dir; on host it may be unwritable or unclear.
4) Playwright launch may fail with `EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/workspace'`.

### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

1.0.7-cli main

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

Linux

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

File "/mnt/disk1/conda_envs/openhands-app/lib/python3.12/multiprocessing/process.py", line 314, in _bootstrap
