all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12025.body:
## Problem

We have a well-established pattern for TanStack Query (https://tanstack.com/query/docs) hooks in the frontend:

- Mutations go in [`frontend/src/hooks/mutation/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation)
- Queries go in [`frontend/src/hooks/query/`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/hooks/query)

However, there are several files with `useMutation` or `useQuery` calls directly in components/routes instead of using dedicated reusable hooks.

### Rogue Usages to Extract

#### `main` branch

https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/33f3861d95ac1c89a6e4f6808f51504ec2e6d9a7/frontend/src/routes/accept-tos.tsx#L26-L59

https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/33f3861d95ac1c89a6e4f6808f51504ec2e6d9a7/frontend/src/components/features/payment/setup-payment-modal.tsx#L13-L21

#### `ALL-2596/org-support` branch

https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/33f3861d95ac1c89a6e4f6808f51504ec2e6d9a7/frontend/src/routes/manage-org.tsx#L93-L98
https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/33f3861d95ac1c89a6e4f6808f51504ec2e6d9a7/frontend/src/routes/manage-org.tsx#L159-L169

## Proposed Solution

Extract these rogue usages to dedicated hooks and move them to the appropriate folders

### Special Cases

[`use-llm-api-key.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-llm-api-key.ts) contains both `useQuery` AND `useMutation`. Suggested to split this into a dedicated query and mutation file instead

## Notes

Since this spans two different branches, it is recommended to open two separate PRs, one merging to their respective branch (one PR merges to `main`, the other to `ALL-2596/org-support`).
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12025.title: Extract rogue `useMutation`/`useQuery` calls into dedicated hooks

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12035.body: ## Problem When a user clicks the pause button in the chat input area (bottom right), the action panel on the right side of the screen incorrectly displays a loading spinner. This happens because the `isPausing` state was included in the `shouldShownAgentLoading` condition, which is shared globally via the conversation store. ### Root Cause The `shouldShownAgentLoading` variable in [`agent-status.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/d772dd65a5e09ea4696d15ad4877c3f642236f79/frontend/src/components/features/controls/agent-status.tsx#L61) serves two purposes: - Local: Controls the loading indicator in the `AgentStatus` component (chat input area) - Global: Synced to `conversation-store` and used by `ConversationTabContent` to show/hide the action panel loading state The `isPausing` flag was included in this shared condition, causing both the local chat UI and the global action panel to show loading states during a pause action. ## Acceptance Criteria - Pause button shows loading indicator in chat input area - Pause button does NOT trigger loading state on action panel - Other loading conditions (`INIT`, `LOADING`, `CONNECTING`, task polling) still work for both areas
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12035.title: Action panel shows loading state when pausing agent

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12043.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

# Performance Issue: Excessive Time Spent on `chown` Operations During SWE-bench Evaluation

## Summary

During SWE-bench evaluation, Docker image building step `#17 [13/22]` takes an excessive amount of time (approximately 41 minutes / 2482.8 seconds) due to multiple `chown -R` operations on large directories.

## Environment

- **OpenHands Version**: v0.62.0
- **Evaluation**: SWE-bench (princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Verified)
- **Agent**: CodeActAgent
- **Docker Build Step**: `#17 [13/22]`

## Problem Description

The Docker build step `#17 [13/22]` in `Dockerfile.j2` contains multiple recursive `chown` operations that take an extremely long time to complete:

```dockerfile
chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/poetry && \
chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/workspace && \
chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/micromamba
```

### Observed Performance

From the build logs:
- **Start Time**: `12:27:11`
- **End Time**: `13:08:34`
- **Duration**: `2482.8s` (approximately **41 minutes**)

This step is significantly slower than other build steps, which typically complete in seconds.

## Expected Behavior

The `chown` operations should complete much faster, ideally within seconds rather than tens of minutes.

## Actual Behavior

The `chown` operations on `/openhands/poetry`, `/openhands/workspace`, and `/openhands/micromamba` take approximately 41 minutes to complete.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run SWE-bench evaluation:
   ```bash
   ./evaluation/benchmarks/swe_bench/scripts/run_infer.sh \
     llm.eval_qwen3_8b HEAD CodeActAgent 500 100 1 \
     princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Verified test
   ```

2. Monitor Docker build logs for step `#17 [13/22]`
3. Observe the excessive time spent on `chown` operations

## Logs

### Relevant Log Entries

```
[92m12:27:11 - openhands:INFO[0m: docker.py:317 - #17 [13/22] RUN ... chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/poetry && ... chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/workspace && chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/micromamba ...
[92m13:08:34 - openhands:INFO[0m: docker.py:317 - #17 DONE 2482.8s
```

### Full Command in Step #17

```bash
RUN /openhands/micromamba/bin/micromamba run -n openhands poetry run python -c "import sys; print('OH_INTERPRETER_PATH=' + sys.executable)" >> /etc/environment && \
    chmod -R 755 /openhands/poetry && \
    chmod -R 755 /openhands/micromamba && \
    chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/poetry && \
    mkdir -p /openhands/workspace && chmod -R g+rws,o+rw /openhands/workspace && \
    chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/workspace && \
    chown -R openhands:openhands /openhands/micromamba && \
    ...
```

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue likely stems from:

1. **Large Directory Trees**: The `/openhands/micromamba` and `/openhands/poetry` directories contain many files (Python packages, conda environments, etc.), making recursive `chown` operations slow.

2. **Filesystem Performance**: The underlying filesystem (possibly overlay2 in Docker) may have performance issues with metadata operations on large directory trees.

3. **Redundant Operations**: Multiple `chown` operations are performed on the same directories at different build steps, which may be unnecessary.

## Suggested Solutions

1. **Use `COPY --chown` instead of `chown`**: When copying files, use Docker's `--chown` flag to set ownership during copy, avoiding post-copy `chown` operations.

2. **Optimize chown operations**: 
   - Only chown directories that actually need it
   - Consider using `find` with `-exec chown` for better performance
   - Use `--no-dereference` flag where appropriate

3. **Reduce chown scope**: 
   - Set ownership at the directory level where possible
   - Avoid recursive chown on entire large directory trees if only specific subdirectories need it

4. **Cache optimization**: Ensure chown operations don't invalidate Docker layer cache unnecessarily

## Related Files

- `openhands/runtime/utils/runtime_templates/Dockerfile.j2` (lines 225-231)
- Multiple `chown` operations throughout the Dockerfile

## Additional Context

- This issue significantly impacts evaluation time, as each instance requires building a new runtime image
- The problem is reproducible across multiple evaluation runs
- Similar performance issues may exist in other build steps with `chown` operations

## Questions

1. Is it necessary to recursively chown all files in `/openhands/micromamba` and `/openhands/poetry`?
2. Could we set ownership during file creation/copy instead of post-processing?
3. Are there any filesystem-specific optimizations we could apply?



### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

Linux

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

_No response_
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12043.title: [Bug]: Excessive Time Spent on `chown` Operations During SWE-bench Evaluation

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

**Problem Description**

When a user manually switches from Basic to Advanced view, updates settings (other than the model), and clicks Save Settings, the view incorrectly switches back to Basic instead of remaining on Advanced.
Root Cause: The useEffect that determines the view runs on every settings change (including after save) and always recalculates based on settings, overriding the user's manual choice.

We can refer to the video below for more information.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6e134cb0-02eb-4a6e-b8fb-dac3f74717be

**Acceptance Criteria:**
 
- When a user manually switches to Advanced view and saves settings, the view should remain on Advanced
- When a user manually switches to Basic view and saves settings, the view should remain on Basic
- The view preference should persist across page refreshes
- Auto-determination should still work on initial load when no preference exists
- Auto-determination should not override a manually set preference

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12109.body:
## Summary

The test `test_tool_call_validation_error_handling` in `tests/unit/controller/test_agent_controller.py` is flaky and fails when run with pytest-xdist (parallel test execution). The test expects 1 `ErrorObservation` but gets multiple (4-10 depending on timing).

## Root Cause Analysis

The test creates an `AgentController` which subscribes to the `EventStream` in its constructor:

```python
if not self.is_delegate:
    self.event_stream.subscribe(
        EventStreamSubscriber.AGENT_CONTROLLER, self.on_event, self.id
    )
```

When `_step()` is called and catches a `BadRequestError` with tool validation failure, it adds an `ErrorObservation` to the event stream:

```python
self.event_stream.add_event(
    ErrorObservation(
        content=f'Tool call validation failed: {str(e)}. Please check the tool parameters and try again.',
    ),
    EventSource.AGENT,
)
```

The `EventStream` has a background thread that processes events and notifies subscribers. When the `ErrorObservation` is added, the background thread calls the controller's `on_event` callback, which then calls `should_step()`. Since `should_step()` returns `True` for `ErrorObservation` events, it triggers another `_step()` call.

This creates a **feedback loop**:
1. Test calls `controller._step()`
2. `_step()` catches `BadRequestError` and adds `ErrorObservation` to event stream
3. Background thread picks up the event and calls `on_event`
4. `on_event` calls `should_step(ErrorObservation)` which returns `True`
5. `on_event` calls `_step_with_exception_handling()`
6. `_step()` catches `BadRequestError` again (mock always raises the same error)
7. Repeat until iteration limit is reached

The test passes without pytest-xdist because the background thread doesn't have time to process events before the assertion runs. With pytest-xdist, the timing is different and the feedback loop executes.

## Proposed Fix

Change the mock in the test to only raise the error once, then return a valid action (like `NullAction`). This makes the test deterministic and correctly tests the error handling logic:

```python
call_count = 0

def mock_step(state):
    nonlocal call_count
    call_count += 1
    if call_count == 1:
        raise BadRequestError(
            message='litellm.BadRequestError: GroqException - {"error":{"message":"tool call validation failed: parameters for tool str_replace_editor did not match schema: errors: [missing properties: \'path\']","type":"invalid_request_error","code":"tool_use_failed"}}',
            model='groq/llama3-8b-8192',
            llm_provider='groq',
        )
    return NullAction()
```

## Related

- Commit that introduced the test: ec9daf3bc "Fix tool call validation error handling for Groq LLM provider (#10927)"
- PR #12107 skips this test as a workaround
- CI failure: https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/actions/runs/20380478897/job/58569463227
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12109.title: [Bug]: Flaky test test_tool_call_validation_error_handling due to feedback loop in EventStream

> [!IMPORTANT]

## Description

During the organization support feature development, several temporary UI components were created as placeholders. These `Temp*` components should be replaced with existing shared/design system components used throughout the application.

### Affected Files

- [`user-context-menu.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/components/features/user/user-context-menu.tsx)
- [`manage-org.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/routes/manage-org.tsx)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Visually inspect the current appearance of all affected components in the UI
- Identify existing shared components that can replace each Temp component
- Replace `TempButton` components with existing button components
- Replace `TempDivider` with existing divider/separator component
- Replace `TempChip` and `TempInteractiveChip` with existing badge/chip components
- Visually verify that the UI looks correct after replacements
- Remove all unused Temp component definitions
-  Confirm all tests pass

### Notes

1. **Before making any changes**, navigate to the user context menu and organization management pages to see the current visual state. After refactoring, perform a visual comparison to ensure the appearance remains consistent or improves.
2. If no suitable replacement component exists for a given Temp component, do not leave it inline. Instead:
    1. Move the component to the appropriate directory (e.g., `frontend/src/components/ui/`)
    2. Rename it appropriately (remove the "Temp" prefix and give it a descriptive name)
    3. Export it properly so it can be reused across the codebase

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12126.body:
> [!IMPORTANT]
> Before starting work on this issue, please ensure that you have thoroughly read the description in #12136.

## Problem

In the `ALL-2596/org-support` branch, we introduced hooks that use TanStack Query for client-side state management. This approach is not inherently an anti-pattern; however, it is inconsistent from our established state management practices in the frontend app.

### Affected Files


- [`use-selected-organization.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/context/use-selected-organization.ts); uses:
  - [`use-selected-organization-id.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-selected-organization-id.ts)
  - [`use-update-selected-organization-id.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/use-update-selected-organization-id.ts)

Problems with the current approach include unnecessary overhead and testing complexity

## Proposed Solution

Refactor to use Zustand for this client-side state, which:
- Is already used elsewhere in the codebase (e.g., [`error-message-store.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/stores/error-message-store.ts), [`optimistic-user-message-store.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/src/stores/optimistic-user-message-store.ts))
- Is purpose-built for client state management
- Provides simpler, more testable code
- **Has clearer semantics for what the code is doing**

## Acceptance Criteria

- New Zustand store created with proper TypeScript types
- Unit tests cover all store functionality (example in [`use-event-store.test.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/ALL-2596/org-support/frontend/__tests__/stores/use-event-store.test.ts))
- All existing consumers work without changes to their interface
- Old TanStack Query hooks are removed
- Route revalidation behavior is preserved (currently triggered in the query hook)

### Note: TDD (optional but encouraged)

This refactor is a good candidate for TDD (Test-Driven Development):

1. **Write tests first** - Define the expected behavior of the Zustand store before implementing it:
    - Initial state should be `null`
    - `setOrgId` should update the state
    - State should persist across hook calls
    - Route revalidation should trigger on state change
2. **Run tests (they will fail)** - Confirm the tests fail with the expected errors
3. **Implement the store** - Write the minimal code to make tests pass
4. **Refactor** - Clean up while keeping tests green
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12126.title: Replace TanStack Query state management with Zustand for selected organization ID

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12170.body:
## Description

When stopping the runtime of a conversation from the conversation panel, the toast confirms success, but the panel continues to show stale data (green dot indicating "RUNNING" instead of grey for "STOPPED”).

We are probably invalidating the query and trigger a refetch too early. This refetch can overwrite the optimistic update with stale server data if the backend hasn't fully propagated the status change yet.

## Acceptance Criteria

> [!NOTE]
> The PR implementing this change should include sufficient test coverage. A test-driven development approach is preferred.

- Do not invalidate query cache after stopping a conversation
- Create or update a test file that:
    - Verifies the optimistic cache update persists after mutation success
    - Ensures no query invalidation overwrites the optimistic status

## Files Affected

- [`use-unified-stop-conversation.ts`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/use-unified-stop-conversation.ts)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12170.title: Conversation panel shows stale status (green dot) after stopping runtime

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12171.body:
## Problem

The Changes tab in the conversation panel has an inconsistent empty state compared to other tabs. When there are no git changes to display, it only shows a `RandomTip` component at the bottom of the screen with nothing in the center.

### Current Behavior

When the Changes tab has no changes to display:
- The main content area is empty
- Only a tip is shown at the bottom
- No icon or message indicating the empty state

### Expected Behavior

The Changes tab should follow the same empty state pattern as other tabs:
- A centered icon
- A descriptive message below the icon

Example from other tabs:
- Browser tab: Globe icon + "No page loaded"
- Planner tab: Lesson plan icon + message + "Create a Plan" button
- Served App tab: Server icon + server message

## Acceptance Criteria

> [!NOTE]
> The PR implementing this change should include sufficient test coverage. A test-driven development approach is preferred.

- Add a centered empty state when there are no git changes
- Display an appropriate icon (suggested: `FaCodeCompare` from `react-icons/fa6`)
- Display a short message (suggested: "No changes yet")
- Keep the `RandomTip` at the bottom (existing behavior)
- Add `i18n` key for the empty state message
- Style should match other empty states

## Relevant Files

- [`changes-tab.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/routes/changes-tab.tsx) - Main file to modify
- [`translation.json`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/i18n/translation.json) - Add translation
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12171.title: Changes tab empty state shows only a tip instead of proper empty state UI

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12172.body:
## Problem

The `TooltipButton` component creates nested interactive elements when used as a wrapper around existing buttons (e.g., `ConversationTabNav`). This results in invalid HTML where a `<button>` is rendered inside another `<button>`:

```tsx
<ChatActionTooltip>
  <ConversationTabNav />  {/* This is a <button> */}
</ChatActionTooltip>
```

Expands to:

```tsx
<Tooltip>
  <button>              {/* TooltipButton creates this */}
    <button>...</button> {/* ConversationTabNav creates this */}
  </button>
</Tooltip>
```

### Symptoms

- Invalid HTML: Buttons cannot contain other buttons per HTML spec
- Broken keyboard navigation: Tab focuses the outer button (which has no action), not the inner one
- Enter key doesn't work: The outer button receives the keypress but has no `onClick` handler
- Double Tab required: Users must `{Tab}` twice to reach the actual actionable button
- Screen reader confusion: Announces nested buttons, which is semantically meaningless

### Why this is bad practice

- `TooltipButton` is overloaded, it conflates two separate concerns:
    - `Tooltip` wrapper: Just show a tooltip on hover
    - Interactive element: A button/link that performs an action
- When a component needs both, `TooltipButton` creates its own interactive element and wraps the child's interactive element, causing the nesting issue.

## Proposed Change

1. Separate the concerns:
    - Create a simple `StyledTooltip` wrapper - Only handles tooltip display with consistent styling (`tooltipClassName`, `closeDelay`, etc.). Does not create any interactive elements.
    - Let components manage their own interactive elements - Each component (button, link, etc.) handles its own click behavior and accessibility attributes.
2. Refactor existing usages:
    - Where `TooltipButton` wraps an existing button -> use `StyledTooltip instead`
    - Where `TooltipButton` creates a button with tooltip -> use `StyledTooltip` + explicit button

## Acceptance Criteria

- No nested `<button>` elements in the DOM
- Keyboard navigation works correctly (single Tab to focus, Enter to activate)
- Screen readers announce elements correctly without nested button confusion
- All existing tooltip functionality preserved (hover, placement, styling)
- Accessibility attributes (`aria-label`, etc.) properly applied to the correct element

## Relevant Files

- [`tooltip-button.tsx`](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/shared/buttons/tooltip-button.tsx)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12172.title: Fix nested button accessibility issue in `TooltipButton` component

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12173.body:
## Problem

The codebase has two different modal systems:
- A HeroUI-based modal at `shared/modals/base-modal/base-modal.tsx` (used only by `MetricsModal`)
- A custom modal system using `ModalBackdrop` + `ModalBody` at `shared/modals/confirmation-modals/base-modal.tsx` (used by most other modals)


The MetricsModal is the outlier, using HeroUI's Modal component with blur backdrop and animations, while the rest of the app uses the custom modal system.

### Why this is bad practice

- Users experience different modal behaviors and appearances throughout the app
- Multiple modal implementations mean duplicated logic and styling that must be maintained separately

## Proposed Changes

Migrate `MetricsModal` to use the custom modal system (`ModalBackdrop` + `ModalBody`) that the rest of the application uses, and remove the HeroUI-based `BaseModal` component if no longer needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Migrate `MetricsModal` to use `ModalBackdrop` and `ModalBody`
- Remove `shared/modals/base-modal/base-modal.tsx` if no other components use it
- Verify modal functionality (open, close, escape key, backdrop click) works correctly
- Visual review to ensure consistent styling with other modals
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12173.title: Standardize modal components to use custom `BaseModal` instead of HeroUI Modal

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12194.body:
## Problem

The [conversation panel](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/conversation-panel.tsx) currently displays a loading spinner while fetching conversations. This creates a jarring user experience because the spinner doesn't reflect the actual layout of the content that will appear, making the transition from loading to loaded state feel abrupt.

## Expected Behavior

The conversation panel should display skeleton cards that match the exact layout of [`ConversationCard`](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/conversation-card/conversation-card.tsx) components during the loading state. This provides users with a visual preview of the content structure and creates a smoother perceived loading experience.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A new `ConversationCardSkeleton` component is created that mirrors the structure of `ConversationCard`.
- The skeleton includes placeholder elements for the status indicator, version badge, title, repository link, and timestamp.
- The skeleton uses the existing `.skeleton` and `.skeleton-round` CSS classes defined in [tailwind.css](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/tailwind.css).
- The `ConversationPanel` component renders multiple skeleton cards instead of a `LoadingSpinner` during initial data fetch.
- The number of skeleton cards displayed should provide adequate visual feedback without excessive rendering.
- The skeleton cards maintain the same dimensions and spacing as actual conversation cards to prevent layout shift.

## Relevant Files

- https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/conversation-panel.tsx is the main panel component that currently uses a loading spinner.
- https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/conversation-card/conversation-card.tsx is the component whose layout the skeleton should match.
- https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/home/recent-conversations/recent-conversations-skeleton.tsx is an existing skeleton implementation that can serve as a reference pattern.
- https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/tailwind.css contains the .skeleton and .skeleton-round CSS classes to be used.

> [!NOTE]
> The PR implementing this change should include sufficient test coverage. A test-driven development approach is preferred.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12194.title: Replace loading spinner with skeleton cards in conversation panel

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12205.body:
## Problem

Some modals in the conversation panel do not pass their cancel/close handler to `ModalBackdrop`, which means users cannot dismiss these modals by clicking outside the modal. This is inconsistent with other modals in the codebase that use the same pattern.

## Expected Behavior

Users should be able to dismiss confirmation modals by clicking outside the modal content, in addition to using the Cancel button.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The `ConfirmDeleteModal` component should pass its `onCancel` prop to `ModalBackdrop` as `onClose`.
- The `ConfirmStopModal ` component should pass its `onCancel` prop to `ModalBackdrop` as `onClose`.
- The `ExitConversationModal ` component should pass its `onCancel` prop to `ModalBackdrop` as `onClose`.
- Clicking the dark backdrop area outside the modal content should close the modal.

## Relevant Files

- [confirm-delete-modal.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/confirm-delete-modal.tsx)
- [confirm-stop-modal.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/confirm-stop-modal.tsx)
- [exit-conversation-modal.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation-panel/exit-conversation-modal.tsx)
- [modal-backdrop.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/shared/modals/modal-backdrop.tsx)

> [!NOTE]
> The PR implementing this change should include sufficient test coverage. A test-driven development approach is preferred.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12205.title: Add missing `onClose` prop to conversation panel modals

A test-driven development approach is preferred.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12209.body:
## Problem

When clicking the refresh button in the Changes tab, there is no visual feedback indicating that the git changes API is being called. Users have no way to know if their click was registered or if data is being fetched, which can lead to confusion and repeated clicks.

## Expected Behavior

The refresh button should provide clear visual feedback when a refresh is in progress. The button should appear in a loading state and prevent additional clicks until the current fetch completes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The refresh icon should spin while the git changes API request is in progress.
- The button should be disabled during loading to prevent multiple simultaneous requests.
- The button should have a visually distinct appearance when disabled, such as reduced opacity.
- Consider using `isFetching` from TanStack Query rather than `isLoading`, since `isLoading` only applies to the initial load while `isFetching` covers refetch scenarios. (see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75401477/reasons-for-using-isloading-or-isfetching-in-react-query)

## Relevant Files

- [frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tab-title.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tab-title.tsx) contains the refresh button component that needs the loading state.
- [frontend/src/hooks/query/use-unified-get-git-changes.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-unified-get-git-changes.ts) contains the hook that fetches git changes and will need to expose isFetching.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12209.title: Add loading feedback to git changes refresh button

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12210.body: ## Problem When returning to an existing conversation, the current loading state displays a `LoadingSpinner` inside the scroll container while the `ChatSuggestions` component remains visible behind it. This creates an awkward visual overlap where users see both the "Build it with AI" suggestions and a loading spinner simultaneously. The `ChatSuggestions` component uses absolute positioning and only hides when `userEventsExist` becomes true, but during the loading phase this condition is `false` because events haven't loaded yet. ## Expected Behavior When loading an existing conversation, the UI should display skeleton placeholder messages that mimic the actual message structure. These skeletons should replace both the loading spinner and the chat suggestions, providing a near-seamless loading experience that accurately represents the content that will appear. ## Acceptance Criteria > [!IMPORTANT] > The PR implementing this change should include sufficient test coverage. A test-driven development approach is preferred. - The loading spinner in the chat interface should be replaced with skeleton message components when loading existing conversations. - The chat suggestions component should be hidden when skeleton loading states are displayed. - Skeleton components should visually match the structure of actual messages, including user messages, assistant messages, and event cards. - The skeleton loading state should only appear when returning to existing conversations, not for new conversations. - Tests should be added for the new skeleton components to verify correct rendering and visibility conditions. ## Relevant Files ### Current loading implementation - [chat-interface.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/chat-interface.tsx) contain the current `LoadingSpinner` implementation. - [chat-suggestions.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/chat-suggestions.tsx) is the component that overlaps with the loading spinner. ### Message components to create skeletons for > [!NOTE] > We do not need individual skeleton components for every message type. Instead, we should create a single composite `ChatMessagesSkeleton` component that renders a realistic-looking mix of placeholder elements, for example one user message skeleton followed by one assistant message skeleton followed by two or three event card skeletons. - [chat-message.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/chat-message.tsx) defines the user and assistant message structure. - [generic-event-message.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/generic-event-message.tsx) defines the event card structure with left border. - [error-message.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/chat/error-message.tsx) defines the error event structure. ### Existing skeleton patterns for reference - [tailwind.css](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/tailwind.css), lines 31-37 define the `.skeleton` and `.skeleton-round` CSS classes. - [recent-conversations-skeleton.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/home/recent-conversations/recent-conversations-skeleton.tsx) is an example of a list skeleton component. - [task-suggestions-skeleton.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/home/tasks/task-suggestions-skeleton.tsx) is an example of a card skeleton component.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12210.title: Replace chat loading spinner with skeleton messages

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12265.body:
## Summary

The runtime fails to export refreshed GitHub tokens when the bash session is busy executing another command. This causes a `RuntimeError: Failed to add env vars [dict_keys(['GITHUB_TOKEN'])] to environment` error.

## Root Cause Analysis

### The Problem

When `_export_latest_git_provider_tokens()` attempts to refresh the GitHub token while the bash session is occupied with a long-running command:

1. Token refresh triggers `add_env_vars()` which creates a `CmdRunAction('export GITHUB_TOKEN=...')`
2. The bash session is busy → no PS1 prompt is visible (0 PS1 metadata blocks detected)
3. After 10 seconds (`NO_CHANGE_TIMEOUT`), `_handle_nochange_timeout_command()` returns `CmdOutputMetadata()` with `exit_code=-1`
4. `add_env_vars()` checks `obs.exit_code != 0` and raises `RuntimeError`

### Code Path

- **base.py:311-315**: `add_env_vars()` creates `CmdRunAction(cmd)` without special timeout handling
- **bash.py:385-400**: `_handle_nochange_timeout_command()` sets `metadata = CmdOutputMetadata()` (default exit_code=-1)
- **action_execution_server.py:274-275,283-284**: `NO_CHANGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` defaults to 10 seconds

### Evidence from Production Logs

Multiple production runtimes show the exact same pattern:

**Runtime 8263dbca**:
- `16:12:42.071` - Token fetched
- `16:12:52.246` - PS1 warning: "Expected exactly one PS1 metadata block BEFORE the execution of a command, but got 0 PS1 metadata blocks"
- `16:12:52.248` - Error raised

**Runtime c394f209**:
- `00:48:16.121` - Token fetched  
- `00:48:26.684` - Error (exact 10s NO_CHANGE_TIMEOUT gap)

All failures show "0 PS1 metadata blocks" indicating the bash session was occupied when the export command was attempted.

## Proposed Fixes

1. **Use `/env` endpoint instead of `CmdRunAction`**: The action execution server has an `/env` endpoint that can set environment variables without using the bash session. This would avoid the race condition entirely.

2. **Add retry logic with backoff**: In `add_env_vars()`, detect the timeout condition and retry after a delay when the bash session becomes available.

3. **Use a separate bash session for env var export**: Create a dedicated bash session for system operations that won't conflict with user commands.

4. **Queue token refresh**: Only attempt token refresh when the bash session is confirmed idle (e.g., check for PS1 prompt availability before executing).

## Affected Code

- `openhands/runtime/base.py` - `add_env_vars()` and `_export_latest_git_provider_tokens()`
- `openhands/runtime/action_execution_server.py` - `NO_CHANGE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`
- `openhands/runtime/impl/action_execution/bash.py` - `_handle_nochange_timeout_command()`

## Labels

bug, runtime
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12265.title: [Bug]: GitHub token export fails when bash session is busy (race condition)

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12272.body:
## Description

When deploying OpenHands with `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS=true` environment variable, the "AI Provider Configuration" modal still appears for users who haven't saved their settings yet.

## Current Behavior

1. Server is configured with `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS=true` in environment variables
2. `/api/options/config` correctly returns `FEATURE_FLAGS.HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS: true`
3. For a new user (or user without saved settings), `/api/settings` returns 404
4. The Sidebar component shows the "AI Provider Configuration" modal despite the feature flag

## Expected Behavior

When `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS=true`, the settings modal should NOT appear, regardless of whether the user has saved settings. The server-side LLM configuration should be used automatically.

## Root Cause

In `frontend/src/components/features/sidebar/sidebar.tsx` (around lines 46-49), the modal display logic does not check the `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS` flag:

```javascript
} else if (config?.APP_MODE === "oss" && settingsError?.status === 404) {
  setSettingsModalIsOpen(true);
}
```

## Suggested Fix

Add a check for `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS` before showing the modal:

```javascript
} else if (
  config?.APP_MODE === "oss" && 
  settingsError?.status === 404 && 
  !config?.FEATURE_FLAGS?.HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS
) {
  setSettingsModalIsOpen(true);
}
```

## Environment

- OpenHands version: 0.41 (Docker image)
- Deployment: Docker Compose with AWS Bedrock backend (server-side LLM configuration)
- `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS=true` set in docker-compose environment

## Workaround

Manually POST to `/api/settings` to save default settings, which prevents the 404 and thus the modal. However, this workaround must be applied per-user.

## Use Case

This issue affects deployments where administrators want to:
- Pre-configure LLM settings server-side (e.g., using AWS Bedrock with IAM roles)
- Prevent users from seeing or modifying LLM configuration
- Provide a seamless experience without requiring users to configure anything
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12272.title: [Bug]: `HIDE_LLM_SETTINGS=true` does not prevent "AI Provider Configuration" modal from appearing

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12287.body: ## Summary The ellipsis dropdown menu in the conversation panel allows users to pin/unpin tabs (Changes, Code, Terminal, App, Browser), but the functionality is broken. Clicking pin/unpin in the dropdown menu has no visible effect on which tabs are displayed. ## Current Behavior There is a `localStorage` key mismatch between the two components that manage tab visibility: - Clicking the ellipsis button opens the dropdown menu correctly. - Clicking pin/unpin on any tab in the dropdown menu has no visible effect. - The tab bar remains unchanged regardless of pin/unpin actions. ### Possible cause The context menu component writes to a hardcoded global localStorage key, while the tabs component reads from a conversation-specific `localStorage` key that includes the `conversationId`. ## Expected Behavior - When a user clicks the ellipsis menu and unpins a tab, that tab should immediately disappear from the visible tabs. - When a user pins a previously unpinned tab, it should reappear in the tab bar. - Pin/unpin preferences should persist correctly per conversation. ## Acceptance Criteria - The context menu and tabs component should use the same `localStorage` key for tracking unpinned tabs. - Pin/unpin actions in the dropdown should immediately reflect in the visible tabs. - Tab visibility preferences should persist across page refreshes. - Unit tests should be added to verify that both components read and write to the same `localStorage` key. - Integration tests should cover the full pin/unpin workflow to prevent regression. ## Relevant Files [conversation-tabs.tsx (lines 48-51)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tabs.tsx#L48-L51) [conversation-tabs-context-menu.tsx (lines 28-31)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tabs-context-menu.tsx#L28-L31)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12287.title: Tab pin/unpin functionality not working in conversation panel dropdown

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12293.body:
## Problem

The frontend stores per-conversation tab preferences in localStorage using conversation-specific keys, but these entries are never removed when conversations are deleted. Over time, this causes unbounded growth of localStorage entries, potentially accumulating hundreds of orphaned entries for active users. Each conversation creates 3 localStorage entries:

- `conversation-selected-tab-${conversationId}`
- `conversation-right-panel-shown-${conversationId}`
- `conversation-unpinned-tabs-${conversationId}`

## Current Behavior

- User opens a conversation → 3 localStorage entries are created
- User deletes the conversation → localStorage entries remain
- Repeat over weeks/months → localStorage accumulates 100s of orphaned entries
- The deletion flow in `useDeleteConversation` only updates TanStack Query cache and calls the backend API. It never cleans up the associated `localStorage` entries.

## Expected Behavior

When a conversation is deleted, the corresponding `localStorage` entries should be removed. Additionally, a one-time migration should clean up existing orphaned entries for users who already have accumulated stale data.

## Acceptance Criteria

- When a conversation is deleted via `useDeleteConversation`, the 3 associated `localStorage` keys should be removed
- A migration utility should clean up orphaned `localStorage` entries (keys where the conversation no longer exists)
- The cleanup should handle edge cases (conversation ID not found, `localStorage` errors)
- No regressions in tab persistence for active conversations

## Relevant Files

[conversation-tabs.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tabs.tsx#L36-L51) - Creates the localStorage entries (lines 36-51)
[use-delete-conversation.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/use-delete-conversation.ts) - Needs cleanup logic added to onSuccess or onSettled
[conversation-tabs-context-menu.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-tabs/conversation-tabs-context-menu.tsx) - Also uses the unpinned tabs key

> [!NOTE]
> Consider creating a utility function (e.g., `clearConversationLocalStorage(conversationId)`) that can be reused and ensures all conversation-related keys are cleaned up in one place.

> [!TIP]
> For the migration cleanup, you could compare `localStorage` keys matching the `conversation-*-${id}` pattern against the user's actual conversation list from the API.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12326.body:
### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

When using `openhands serve --mount-cwd`, the current working directory is not mounted into the agent-server container. The CLI correctly sets the `SANDBOX_VOLUMES` environment variable, but the new agent-server architecture does not read this variable when creating Docker containers.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Navigate to a directory with files: `cd /path/to/my/project`
2. Run: `openhands serve --mount-cwd`
3. Start a new conversation in the UI
4. Ask the agent to list files in the current directory
5. The agent will not see the files from your local directory

**Expected behavior:**
The files from the current working directory should be visible at `/workspace` inside the agent-server container.

**Actual behavior:**
The `/workspace` directory in the agent-server container is empty or contains default files, not the mounted directory.

### Root Cause

The migration from the old runtime architecture (V0) to the new agent-server architecture (V1) did not include handling for the `SANDBOX_VOLUMES` environment variable.

In `openhands/app_server/config.py`, the `config_from_env()` function creates a `DockerSandboxServiceInjector` but does not parse `SANDBOX_VOLUMES` to create `VolumeMount` objects.

The CLI sets:
```
-e SANDBOX_VOLUMES=/your/cwd:/workspace:rw
```

But this is never read by the app server when creating agent-server containers.

### OpenHands Installation

CLI (`openhands serve`)

### OpenHands Version

latest

### Operating System

Linux

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

You can verify the issue by:
1. Running `docker exec openhands-app env | grep SANDBOX` - shows `SANDBOX_VOLUMES` is set
2. Running `docker inspect <agent-server-container> | grep -A 20 Mounts` - shows no mount for the user's directory
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12326.title: [Bug]: --mount-cwd flag does not mount directory in agent-server container

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12344.body:
## Bug Description

V1 conversations fail to restore from persistence with a Pydantic validation error when the conversation state contains secrets in the `secret_registry`. The error occurs because secrets are serialized as redacted values (`**********`) but the `StaticSecret` model requires a non-null `value` field.

## Error Message

```
pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 6 validation errors for ConversationState
secret_registry.secret_sources.LLM_API_KEY.value
  Field required [type=missing, input_value={'description': 'This is ...oxy.app.all-hands.dev/'}, input_type=dict]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/missing
secret_registry.secret_sources.DD_API_KEY.value
  Field required [type=missing, input_value={'description': 'Datadog API Key'}, input_type=dict]
...
```

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue is in the `openhands-sdk` package, specifically in how secrets are serialized and deserialized:

1. **Serialization** (`state.py` line 161-162):
   ```python
   def _save_base_state(self, fs: FileStore) -> None:
       payload = self.model_dump_json(exclude_none=True)
   ```
   This calls `model_dump_json()` without providing a `cipher` or `expose_secrets=True` in the context.

2. **Secret Serialization** (`pydantic_secrets.py` line 6-28):
   ```python
   def serialize_secret(v: SecretStr | None, info):
       if info.context and info.context.get("cipher"):
           return cipher.encrypt(v)
       if info.context and info.context.get("expose_secrets"):
           return v.get_secret_value()
       return v  # Returns SecretStr, which Pydantic serializes as "**********"
   ```

3. **Secret Deserialization** (`pydantic_secrets.py` line 31-64):
   ```python
   def validate_secret(v: str | SecretStr | None, info) -> SecretStr | None:
       if not secret_value or not secret_value.strip() or secret_value == "**********":
           return None  # Redacted secrets become None
   ```

4. **StaticSecret Model** (`secrets.py` line 25-28):
   ```python
   class StaticSecret(SecretSource):
       value: SecretStr  # REQUIRED field, not Optional
   ```

When the conversation is restored, the redacted `"**********"` value is converted to `None`, but `StaticSecret.value` is a required field, causing validation to fail.

## Timeline from Datadog Logs

| Time (UTC) | Event |
|------------|-------|
| 15:57:20 | Conversation `eb4d72fc-37c2-4316-a3e7-90421ba22e0a` created successfully |
| 16:38:07 | Conversation working normally (API calls returning 200) |
| **17:03:58** | **ERROR**: `error_loading_event_service` - Pydantic validation failed |
| 17:04:04+ | All subsequent requests return 404 "Conversation not found" |

## Affected Versions

- `openhands-sdk` 1.7.4 (confirmed)
- `openhands-sdk` 1.8.1 (confirmed - same code)

## Suggested Fix

Option 1: Make `StaticSecret.value` optional with a default of `None`:
```python
class StaticSecret(SecretSource):
    value: SecretStr | None = None
```

Option 2: Always use a cipher when serializing conversation state to preserve secrets.

Option 3: Skip serializing secrets entirely and require them to be re-provided on conversation restore.

## Impact

- Users lose access to V1 conversations after server restarts or reconnections
- The conversation data is still on disk but cannot be loaded
- Error message "Connection lost: Conversation not found" is shown to users

## Related

- Conversation ID: `eb4d72fc-37c2-4316-a3e7-90421ba22e0a`
- URL: https://app.all-hands.dev/conversations/eb4d72fc37c24316a3e790421ba22e0a
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12344.title: [Bug] V1 Conversation fails to restore due to secret serialization/deserialization mismatch

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12358.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

### Description

I am encountering a persistent `500 Internal Server Error` when trying to start a conversation/task using a repository from a self-hosted Gitea instance (configured via the Forgejo provider).

While the OpenHands UI successfully connects to the Gitea API (listing repositories and branches works perfectly), the **Sandbox Container** fails immediately upon initialization. The logs indicate that the internal Git client inside the sandbox is forcing an **HTTPS** connection for the `git clone` operation, even though the provider is explicitly configured for **HTTP**.

Because the Gitea server is running on plain HTTP, the Sandbox's attempt to initiate a TLS handshake results in a crash, which bubbles up as a 500 error in the API.

### Environment

* **Host OS:** Linux Mint Debian Edition (virtualized in Proxmox)
* **Deployment:** Docker (Standard `docker run` command)
* **OpenHands Version:** 1.1 (Image: `docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:1.1`)
* **Git Provider:** Self-hosted Gitea (accessed via local IP `192.168.178.120:3000`)
* **Provider Setting:** Using "Forgejo" (since Gitea is compatible) - Note: The UI successfully lists all Gitea repositories and allows selection before the crash occurs during project creation.
* **Protocol:** HTTP (No SSL/TLS configured on Gitea)

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run a self-hosted Gitea instance on HTTP (e.g., `http://192.168.x.x:3000`).
2. Start OpenHands via Docker with `--add-host` to ensure connectivity.
3. In OpenHands UI -> Settings, configure the **Forgejo** provider with the Base URL `http://192.168.x.x:3000` and a valid token.
4. Select a repository from the dropdown (successfully lists repos).
5. Start a new conversation/task.

### Expected Behavior

The Sandbox container should respect the protocol defined in the Git Provider settings (HTTP) and successfully clone the repository via `http://...`.

### Actual Behavior

The UI prepares the task, but the Sandbox container immediately crashes. The `openhands-app` logs show a `500 Internal Server Error` on `/api/conversations`. Deep diving into the logs reveals a `GnuTLS handshake failed` error, indicating an HTTPS request was sent to an HTTP port.

### Logs

**1. The 500 API Error:**

```text
httpx.HTTPStatusError: Server error '500 Internal Server Error' for url 'http://host.docker.internal:xxxxx/api/conversations'
...
openhands.app_server.sandbox.docker_sandbox_service:INFO: docker_sandbox_service.py:202 - Sandbox server not running: http://host.docker.internal:xxxxx : Server disconnected without sending a response.

```

**2. The Root Cause (Git Protocol Mismatch):**

```text
openhands.app_server.app_conversation.app_conversation_service_base:WARNING: app_conversation_service_base.py:291 - Git clone failed:
Cloning into 'deepwiki-open-fixxed'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://192.168.178.120:3000/master/deepwiki-open-fixxed.git/': GnuTLS, handshake failed: An unexpected TLS packet was received.

```

*(Note: The log explicitly shows `https://` being used, despite the configuration being `http://`)*

**3. Config File Ignored:**

```text
openhands:WARNING: utils.py:171 - No [core] section found in config.toml. Core settings will use defaults.
openhands:WARNING: utils.py:373 - Unknown section [git] in config.toml

```

### Attempted Solutions (All Failed)

We have tried extensive troubleshooting to rule out environment issues:

1. **UI Configuration:** Explicitly set the Forgejo URL to `http://...`. **Result:** Ignored by the Sandbox Git client.
2. **Environment Variables:**
* `GIT_SSL_NO_VERIFY=true`: **Result:** Failed. (The issue is a protocol mismatch/handshake failure, not an invalid certificate, so skipping verification doesn't help).


3. **Config.toml:**
* Mounted a `config.toml` with `[git] force_http = true`.
* **Result:** Failed. The logs show `Unknown section [git] in config.toml`, suggesting this configuration method is deprecated or not working in version 1.1.


4. **Global Git Config Injection:**
* Attempted to run `git config --system url."http://...".insteadOf "https://..."` inside the running container.
* **Result:** Failed to persist or apply to the spawned sandbox environment.



### Analysis

The issue appears to be that the **Sandbox Runtime** does not inherit or respect the `http` protocol defined in the OpenHands provider settings. It defaults to `https` for the `git clone` operation. Since the target server is strictly HTTP, the TLS handshake fails, causing the git process to exit with a fatal error, which in turn causes the Sandbox server to crash, resulting in the generic 500 error in the main application.


### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

latest

### Model Name

ollama/devstral-small-2:24b-instruct-2512-q8_0

### Operating System

Linux

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context
> [!IMPORTANT]
> IP addresses and repository names - as well as any other private or local information that is irrelevant - have been censored.

```
Starting OpenHands...
22:46:21 - openhands:WARNING: utils.py:171 - No [core] section found in config.toml. Core settings will use defaults.
22:46:21 - openhands:WARNING: utils.py:373 - Unknown section [git] in config.toml
22:46:21 - openhands:INFO: server_config.py:63 - Using config class None
INFO: Started server process [8]
```

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12366.body:
**What problem or use case are you trying to solve?**

Currently, we include the following checklist in each pull request description to ensure that the author has personally reviewed and tested their changes:

```
## Checklist
<!-- AI/LLM AGENTS: This checklist is for a human author to complete. Do NOT check either of the two boxes below. Leave them unchecked until a human has personally reviewed and tested the changes. -->

- [ ] I have read and reviewed the code and I understand what the code is doing.
- [ ] I have tested the code to the best of my ability and ensured it works as expected.
```

While this helps confirm review and testing, it would be even more effective if we also encouraged authors to provide a visual demonstration of their work, such as screenshots or a short video. This can make it easier for reviewers to understand the changes and verify the behavior.

For this reason, we would like to update our pull request template to include an additional optional section named “Demo Screenshots/Videos.”

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- A new section titled “Demo Screenshots/Videos” appears in every pull request description, allowing authors to attach screenshots or videos that demonstrate their changes.

**Describe the UX or technical implementation you have in mind**

We update our pull request template to include an additional optional section named “Demo Screenshots/Videos.”

**Additional context**
N/A.

### If you find this feature request or enhancement useful, make sure to add a 👍 to the issue

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12397.body:
## Summary

Customer reports receiving a "read:me scope is required" error when setting up Jira Cloud integration, even after adding the scope to their Atlassian app configuration.

**Screenshot from customer:**
The error message states: "This app has requested Jira API & User identity API scopes that have not been added to the app. Configure the app at https://developer.atlassian.com/apps and add the following scopes to the Jira API & User identity API: read:me."

## Analysis

After investigating the codebase and Atlassian OAuth documentation, I identified **two potential issues** that may be causing this problem:

### 1. Most Likely: App Configuration Issue on Atlassian Side

The `read:me` scope belongs to the **User Identity API**, not the **Jira API**. In the Atlassian Developer Console, these are configured in **separate sections**:

- **User Identity API** → requires `read:me` scope
- **Jira API** → requires `read:jira-user`, `read:jira-work`, etc.

**Evidence:**
- According to [Commvault documentation on Atlassian OAuth scopes](https://documentation.commvault.com/11.42/software/creating_oauth_app_for_atlassian_jira_with_required_scopes.html), the `read:me` scope must be added specifically under "User Identity API" in Permissions.
- The error message explicitly mentions "Jira API & User identity API" - indicating the customer may have only added scopes to the Jira API section.

**Customer troubleshooting steps:**
1. Go to https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/
2. Select the OAuth app
3. Navigate to **Permissions**
4. Under **User identity API**, verify that "View active user profile" (`read:me`) is enabled
5. Under **Jira API**, verify classic scopes `read:jira-user` and `read:jira-work` are enabled
6. After making changes, users who previously authorized the app may need to **revoke and re-authorize** to pick up the new scopes

### 2. Secondary Concern: Missing URL Encoding in OAuth Authorization URL

I noticed that the OAuth authorization URL construction does not properly URL-encode the query parameters:

**Current code** (enterprise/server/routes/integration/jira.py, lines 301-313):
```python
auth_params = {
    'audience': 'api.atlassian.com',
    'client_id': JIRA_CLIENT_ID,
    'scope': JIRA_SCOPES,  # 'read:me read:jira-user read:jira-work'
    'redirect_uri': JIRA_REDIRECT_URI,
    'state': state,
    'response_type': 'code',
    'prompt': 'consent',
}

auth_url = (
    f"{JIRA_AUTH_URL}?{'&'.join([f'{k}={v}' for k, v in auth_params.items()])}"
)
```

This produces a URL with **unencoded spaces** in the scope parameter:
```
https://auth.atlassian.com/authorize?...&scope=read:me read:jira-user read:jira-work&...
```

According to [Atlassian's OAuth 3LO documentation](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/getting-started/implementing-oauth-3lo/), the scopes should be URL-encoded with `%20` between them:
```
scope=REQUESTED_SCOPE_ONE%20REQUESTED_SCOPE_TWO
```

While browsers may handle unencoded spaces, this could cause parsing issues where only `read:me` is recognized as the scope (truncated at the first space).

**Recommended fix:**
```python
from urllib.parse import urlencode
auth_url = f'{JIRA_AUTH_URL}?{urlencode(auth_params)}'
```

## Why `read:me` is Required

The `read:me` scope is used in the OAuth callback flow to retrieve the Jira user's account ID:

**enterprise/server/routes/integration/jira.py:457-465:**
```python
jira_user_response = requests.get(JIRA_USER_INFO_URL, headers=headers)  # https://api.atlassian.com/me
if jira_user_response.status_code != 200:
    raise HTTPException(
        status_code=400,
        detail=f'Error fetching user info: {jira_user_response.text}',
    )

jira_user_info = jira_user_response.json()
jira_user_id = jira_user_info.get('account_id')
```

The `account_id` is then stored to link the Jira user to their OpenHands account. Without this scope, the `/me` endpoint call will fail.

## Relevant Code

- **Scopes defined:** enterprise/server/routes/integration/jira.py:28
- **Authorization URL construction:** enterprise/server/routes/integration/jira.py:301-313 and 363-373
- **User info endpoint usage:** enterprise/server/routes/integration/jira.py:457-465

**Git blame:** All code authored by Ray Myers in commit e37f7b0e0 ("Enterprise code and docker build (#10770)", 2025-09-04)

## Recommended Actions

1. **Immediate customer guidance:** Share troubleshooting steps for verifying the User Identity API scope configuration in Atlassian Developer Console
2. **Code fix consideration:** Add proper URL encoding using `urllib.parse.urlencode()` for the authorization URL construction (both in jira.py and jira_dc.py)
3. **Documentation:** Consider adding setup documentation for Jira Cloud integration that explicitly mentions the need to configure scopes in both User Identity API and Jira API sections

## References

- [Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) Implementation Guide](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/getting-started/implementing-oauth-3lo/)
- [Jira scopes for OAuth 2.0 (3LO) and Forge apps](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/scopes-for-oauth-2-3LO-and-forge-apps/)
- [Example: Creating OAuth App with Required Scopes](https://documentation.commvault.com/11.42/software/creating_oauth_app_for_atlassian_jira_with_required_scopes.html)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12397.title: Jira Cloud Integration: read:me scope error even after customer adds scope

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12403.body:
## Summary

When deploying OpenHands behind a reverse proxy (nginx/CloudFront), accessing user-launched applications (e.g., Flask apps on port 5000) via `/runtime/{port}/` URLs results in 502 Bad Gateway errors. This is due to V1's `DockerSandboxService` architecture limitations.

## Problem Description

### Current V1 Architecture Limitations

1. **Fixed exposed ports**: V1 only exposes 4 hardcoded ports (8000, 8001, 8011, 8012) in `sandbox_models.py`
2. **No `network_mode` support**: `DockerSandboxService` doesn't support host networking
3. **Dynamic port mapping**: Bridge network uses dynamic host ports, making it incompatible with proxy patterns like `localhost:{container_port}`

### How this affects users

When a user asks the agent to "Create a Flask app and run it on port 5000":
1. The Flask app starts inside the sandbox container on port 5000
2. Port 5000 is NOT in the exposed ports list (`ExposedPortName`)
3. The port is never mapped to the host
4. The reverse proxy (`/runtime/5000/` → `localhost:5000`) fails with 502

### V0 vs V1 Comparison

| Feature | V0 (DockerRuntime) | V1 (DockerSandboxService) |
|---------|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Network Mode | ✅ Configurable via `SANDBOX_DOCKER_RUNTIME_KWARGS` | ❌ Hardcoded bridge |
| Exposed Ports | ✅ Dynamic based on app | ❌ Fixed (8000, 8001, 8011, 8012) |
| Runtime URL Access | ✅ Works with host networking | ❌ 502 errors |

## Proposed Solutions

### Option 1: Add `network_mode` support (Recommended)

Allow `DockerSandboxService` to use host networking via configuration:

```python
# In docker_sandbox_service.py
network_mode = sandbox_config.use_host_network and 'host' or 'bridge'
container = self.docker_client.containers.run(
    ...,
    network_mode=network_mode,
    ...
)
```

**Note**: The `use_host_network` field already exists in `sandbox_config.py` but isn't used.

### Option 2: Add configurable extra ports

Allow users to specify additional ports to expose:

```toml
# config.toml
[sandbox]
extra_exposed_ports = [5000, 8080]
```

### Option 3: Add common development ports to ExposedPortName

```python
class ExposedPortName(StrEnum):
    AGENT_SERVER = 'agent_server'  # 8000
    VSCODE = 'vscode'              # 8001
    WORKER_1 = 'worker_1'          # 8011
    WORKER_2 = 'worker_2'          # 8012
    # Add common development ports
    FLASK_DEFAULT = 'flask_default'  # 5000
    DJANGO_DEFAULT = 'django_default'  # 8000 (already covered)
    NODE_DEFAULT = 'node_default'  # 3000
```

## Environment

- OpenHands Version: Latest main branch (V1 app server)
- Deployment: AWS EC2 with CloudFront + nginx reverse proxy
- Sandbox Type: Docker

## Related Code

- `openhands/app_server/sandbox/docker_sandbox_service.py` - Container creation (no `network_mode` param)
- `openhands/app_server/sandbox/sandbox_models.py` - `ExposedPortName` enum (fixed ports)
- `openhands/core/config/sandbox_config.py` - Has `use_host_network` field but unused

## Workaround

Currently, users can instruct the agent to run apps on port 8011 (WORKER_1) or 8012 (WORKER_2) instead of the default Flask port 5000. However, this is not intuitive and breaks common development patterns.

## Additional Context

This issue was discovered while deploying OpenHands with a CDK-based infrastructure that uses CloudFront → ALB → nginx → Docker architecture. The nginx runtime proxy pattern (`/runtime/{port}/` → `localhost:{port}`) works correctly for V0 with `SANDBOX_DOCKER_RUNTIME_KWARGS={"network_mode":"host"}`, but V1 ignores this environment variable.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12403.title: [Feature Request] V1 DockerSandboxService: Support for host networking and additional exposed ports

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12413.body:
## Problem

Users working on web applications are confused about how to get their apps to display in the **Browser** and **App** tabs in the OpenHands UI. The current empty state messages are minimal and do not provide enough guidance:

- **App tab**: Shows "start a web server to run the application" which is vague
- **Browser tab**: Shows "no page loaded" without any helpful instructions

## User Feedback

> "The UI is great that it lets us use the browser and view the app with webserver but there arent really any instructions on how to get OpenHands to render/run either/or respective to the given project. Im working on a web app, not really sure how to get it to display the browser."
>
> "Also stating app might be confusing for someone working on a web app, what does this actually render? Like phone apps?"
>
> "Possibly including some more info on the unloaded app/web tabs that explains how to get them to run in OpenHands would benefit others."

## Proposed Solution

### Browser Tab
- Improve the empty state to include actionable instructions like:
  - "Ask OpenHands to navigate to a URL or start browsing"
  - "The agent can browse websites to research information or test web applications"
  - Example prompts users can try

### App Tab
This overlaps with #12049 which proposes excellent improvements including:
- Renaming "App" to "Web App" for clarity
- Providing clear empty-state messaging
- Adding troubleshooting hints

### General Improvements
- Add brief explanations of what each tab is for (tooltip or in empty state)
- Include example prompts or quick-start instructions
- Link to documentation for detailed setup instructions

## Related Issues
- #12049 - [App tab] Improve ability for Agent to use the App tab

## Additional Context
This would particularly help new users who are trying OpenHands for web development projects and are not sure how to leverage these features.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12413.title: Improve Browser and App tab empty state instructions for better user guidance

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12421.body:
## Overview

Migrate the OpenHands project from Poetry to UV for Python dependency management. UV offers significantly faster dependency resolution and installation times, which will improve developer experience and CI/CD performance.

## Why UV?

- **10-100x faster** dependency resolution and installation
- **Better CI/CD performance** - faster builds and tests
- **Modern PEP 621 compliance** - uses standard `[project]` metadata
- **Drop-in replacement** - similar commands to Poetry

## Migration Plan

### Phase 1: Non-Breaking Changes (Add UV Support)

- [x] Step 1.1: Add PEP 621 `[project]` section (#12414)
- [x] Step 1.2: Add `[dependency-groups]` section (#12415)
- [x] Steps 1.3-1.6: Add `[project.dependencies]`, `[project.optional-dependencies]`, generate `uv.lock` (#12416)
- [ ] Step 2: Add UV support to Makefile (alongside Poetry)
- [ ] Step 3: Add UV support to Docker files (alongside Poetry)
- [ ] Step 4: Add UV support to GitHub Actions (alongside Poetry)
- [ ] Step 5: Update runtime build code to support both lock files
- [ ] Step 6: Update evaluation scripts to support both tools
- [ ] Step 7: Update documentation
- [ ] Step 8: Apply same changes to enterprise/pyproject.toml

### Phase 2: Breaking Changes (Switch to UV as Primary)

- [ ] Change build-system from `poetry.core.masonry.api` to `hatchling`
- [ ] Update `build.sh` to use `uv build`
- [ ] Switch Makefile to use UV by default
- [ ] Switch Docker files to use UV by default
- [ ] Switch GitHub Actions to use UV by default
- [ ] Update all scripts from `poetry run` to `uv run`

### Phase 3: Cleanup

- [ ] Remove `[tool.poetry]` sections from pyproject.toml
- [ ] Remove `poetry.lock`
- [ ] Remove Poetry installation from Docker/CI

## Reference

- The `openhands-cli` subdirectory already uses UV (`uv.lock` exists) and can serve as a reference implementation
- UV documentation: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/

## Related PRs

- #12414 - Step 1.1: Add PEP 621 `[project]` section
- #12415 - Step 1.2: Add `[dependency-groups]` section
- #12416 - Steps 1.3-1.6: Add dependencies and generate `uv.lock`
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12421.title: Migrate from Poetry to UV for Python dependency management

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12464.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

I encountered the error below while running locally in SaaS mode.

```
{
  "ts": "2026-01-06T18:03:15.331406",
  "message": "error_reading_from_redis",
  "severity": "ERROR",
  "exc_info": [
    "Traceback (most recent call last):",
    "  File 'enterprise/server/clustered_conversation_manager.py', line 425, in _update_state_in_redis_task",
    "    await self._update_state_in_redis()",
    "  File 'enterprise/server/clustered_conversation_manager.py', line 452, in _update_state_in_redis",
    "    async for key in redis.scan_iter(pattern):",
    "                     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^",
    "AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'scan_iter'"
  ]
}
```

### Root Cause Analysis

**Root cause:** Version mismatch in python-socketio between poetry.lock and enterprise/poetry.lock.

### Findings

1. Version difference:
- poetry.lock: python-socketio 5.13.0
- enterprise/poetry.lock: python-socketio 5.16.0
- Redis version: 6.4.0 (same in both)

2. Error location:
- File: enterprise/server/clustered_conversation_manager.py
- Line 452: async for key in redis.scan_iter(pattern):
- redis is None because _get_redis_client() returns None

3. Redis client access:

```
def _get_redis_client(self):
    return getattr(self.sio.manager, 'redis', None)
```

This accesses self.sio.manager.redis, which is None in the current branch.

### Solution

- Update python-socketio = "5.13.0"

### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

None

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

_No response_

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12489.body:
## Summary of PR

This PR adds CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) support to the Docker sandbox service, matching the existing implementation in the remote sandbox service. This fixes an issue where users running OpenHands via Docker on a remote server (e.g., Ubuntu Server VM) would encounter WebSocket connection failures when accessing the UI from an external machine.

**Changes:**
- Added `ALLOW_CORS_ORIGINS_VARIABLE` constant (`OH_ALLOW_CORS_ORIGINS_0`) to docker_sandbox_service.py
- Added `web_url` field to `DockerSandboxService` dataclass
- Updated `start_sandbox()` to set CORS origins environment variable when `web_url` is configured
- Updated `DockerSandboxServiceInjector` to retrieve `web_url` from global config and pass it to the service
- Added unit tests for CORS functionality

When users configure `WEB_HOST` or `web_url`, the Docker sandbox service will now pass the `OH_ALLOW_CORS_ORIGINS_0` environment variable to agent-server containers, allowing them to accept requests from remote browsers.

This complements the existing `SANDBOX_CONTAINER_URL_PATTERN` environment variable for remote access scenarios.

## Demo Screenshots/Videos

N/A - Backend infrastructure change

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Other (dependency update, docs, typo fixes, etc.)

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read and reviewed the code and I understand what the code is doing.
- [ ] I have tested the code to the best of my ability and ensured it works as expected.

## Fixes

Resolves the issue discussed in Discord where users running OpenHands on a remote server couldn't access from external browsers due to CORS errors with WebSocket connections.

## Release Notes

- [x] Include this change in the Release Notes.

Added CORS support to Docker sandbox service for remote browser access. Users running OpenHands on remote servers should set `WEB_HOST` environment variable (e.g., `WEB_HOST=192.168.1.100:3000`) along with `SANDBOX_CONTAINER_URL_PATTERN` for proper remote access configuration.

@tofarr can click here to [continue refining the PR](https://app.all-hands.dev/conversations/None)

---

To run this PR locally, use the following command:

GUI with Docker:
```
docker run -it --rm   -p 3000:3000   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock   --add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway   -e SANDBOX_RUNTIME_CONTAINER_IMAGE=docker.openhands.dev/openhands/runtime:b0a69e7-nikolaik   --name openhands-app-b0a69e7   docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:b0a69e7
```
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12489.title: Add CORS origins support to Docker sandbox service for remote browser access
