all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12526.body: ### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead). - [x] I have checked the existing issues. ### Describe the bug and reproduction steps ## Describe the bug When starting a new conversation in OpenHands (Docker Compose deployment), the sandbox fails to start with timeout error. The runtime container (`ghcr.io/openhands/runtime:1.2-nikolaik`) exits immediately with error code 127. ## Error Message **UI Error:** ``` 500: Sandbox failed to start within 120s: oh-agent-server-XXXXXXX ``` **Runtime Container Logs:** ``` [FATAL tini (6)] exec --port failed: No such file or directory ``` ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Clone the OpenHands repository 2. Run with Docker Compose: ```bash LLM_API_KEY="" \ LLM_MODEL="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929" \ docker compose up --build ``` 3. Open http://localhost:3000 4. Try to start a new conversation 5. Error appears after ~120 seconds ## Expected Behavior The sandbox/runtime container should start successfully and allow conversations to begin. ## Environment | Component | Version | |-----------|---------| | OS | Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS (Noble Numbat) | | Kernel | 6.8.0-90-generic x86_64 | | Docker | 29.1.5 | | OpenHands Version | 1.2.1 | | Runtime Image | `ghcr.io/openhands/runtime:1.2-nikolaik` | ## Container Status ``` CONTAINER ID IMAGE STATUS 1de4779e83e0 ghcr.io/openhands/runtime:1.2-nikolaik Exited (127) ``` ## Additional Context - The same issue occurs with both `docker.openhands.dev/openhands/runtime:1.2-nikolaik` and `ghcr.io/openhands/runtime:1.2-nikolaik` - Exit code 127 typically indicates "command not found" - The error suggests the entrypoint or command in the runtime image is misconfigured - Running `make run` (non-Docker) also eventually hits similar MCP timeout issues ## Possible Cause The runtime container's entrypoint appears to be looking for a `--port` executable rather than passing `--port` as an argument to the actual entrypoint command. ### OpenHands Installation Docker command in README ### OpenHands Version 1.2.1 ### Model Name anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 ### Operating System Linux ### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12526.title: [Bug]: Sandbox fails to start: runtime container exits with "exec --port failed: No such file or directory"

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12551.body:
## Problem

The frontend contains several subscription-related files that are dead code - they are not imported or used anywhere in the application. This unused code increases maintenance burden, contributes to bundle size, and could cause confusion for developers.

### Why this is bad practice

* Maintenance burden - developers may waste time updating unused code
* Bundle size - unused code increases the JavaScript bundle unnecessarily
* Developer confusion - contributors may think these features are active
* Test coverage - unused code may not be covered by tests and could rot silently

## Files to Remove

**Components:**
* [cancel-subscription-modal.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/payment/cancel-subscription-modal.tsx) - Modal component that is never rendered

**Hooks:**
* [use-create-subscription-checkout-session.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/stripe/use-create-subscription-checkout-session.ts) - Mutation hook never called
* [use-cancel-subscription.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/mutation/use-cancel-subscription.ts) - Only imported by unused modal
* [use-subscription-access.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-subscription-access.ts) - Query hook never called

**Types (in `billing.types.ts`):**
* `SubscriptionAccess` type
* `CancelSubscriptionResponse` type

**API methods (in `billing-service.api.ts`):**
* `getSubscriptionAccess()` - lines 52-57
* `createSubscriptionCheckoutSession()` - lines 63-70
* `cancelSubscription()` - lines 76-81

**i18n entries (in `translation.json`):**
* `PAYMENT$CANCEL_SUBSCRIPTION_MESSAGE`
* `PAYMENT$CANCEL_SUBSCRIPTION_MESSAGE_WITH_DATE`
* `PAYMENT$SUBSCRIPTION_CANCELLED_EXPIRES`

## Acceptance Criteria

* Remove all files listed above
* Remove unused methods from `BillingService` class
* Remove unused types from `billing.types.ts`
* Remove unused i18n translation keys
* Build completes without errors (`npm run build`)
* All tests pass (`npm run test`)
* No TypeScript errors

> [!NOTE]
> The following payment files are actively used and should **NOT** be removed:
> - `setup-payment-modal.tsx` (used in `root-layout.tsx`)
> - `payment-form.tsx` (used in `billing.tsx`)
> - `powered-by-stripe-tag.tsx` (used in `payment-form.tsx`)
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12551.title: Remove unused subscription-related code

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12552.body: ## Problem The chat interface has a status row area above the chat input (containing the scroll-to-bottom button, typing indicator, and status indicators). This row has no explicit background, causing the parent's dark `bg-base` (`#0d0f11`) to show through. This creates a visual "dark bar" that makes message content appear to be hidden/cut off prematurely before it actually scrolls behind the chat input box. ## Current Behavior 1. User scrolls up in the chat conversation 2. Message content at the bottom gets clipped at the scroll container's bottom edge 3. The status row (containing scroll-to-bottom button, typing indicator, etc.) sits below the scroll container 4. The gap between the scroll container bottom and chat input top shows the parent's dark `bg-base` (`#0d0f11`) background 5. This creates a visible dark rectangular area that appears to "hide" text content before it actually goes behind the chat input (`bg-[#25272D]`) The visual effect is jarring because there are two different dark colors: - `bg-base`: `#0d0f11` (nearly black) - showing through the status row - Chat input: `bg-[#25272D]` (dark charcoal) - the actual input container ## Expected Behavior The transition from the message scroll area to the chat input should be visually seamless. Text content should only appear to be hidden once it actually scrolls behind the chat input box, not in an intermediate dark area above it. ## Acceptance Criteria * The status row area above the chat input should not create a visible dark "bar" effect * The visual transition from messages to chat input should be smooth and consistent * The scroll-to-bottom button, typing indicator, and status indicators should remain functional and visible * No regressions in chat scrolling behavior or message visibility ## Relevant Files * [chat-interface.tsx (lines 315-353)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/60e668f4a7872c6c3e6e5a0597a9c90781576a47/frontend/src/components/features/chat/chat-interface.tsx#L315-L353) - Contains the bottom section with the status row that lacks a background * [chat-input-container.tsx (line 56)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/60e668f4a7872c6c3e6e5a0597a9c90781576a47/frontend/src/components/features/chat/components/chat-input-container.tsx#L56) - Chat input with `bg-[#25272D]` * [desktop-layout.tsx (line 32)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/60e668f4a7872c6c3e6e5a0597a9c90781576a47/frontend/src/components/features/conversation/conversation-main/desktop-layout.tsx#L32) - Parent container with `bg-base` * [tailwind.css (line 16)](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/60e668f4a7872c6c3e6e5a0597a9c90781576a47/frontend/src/tailwind.css#L16) - Defines `--color-base: #0d0f11`
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12552.title: Fix dark background in chat status row hiding message content before scrolling behind input

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12553.title: RFC: Standardize mutation toast handling with utility helper

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12555.body: ## Problem In OSS mode, when a user has no Git providers configured, the "Suggested Tasks" section on the home screen displays "No tasks available" without any context. This message doesn't explain **why** there are no tasks or guide the user toward resolving the issue. The current implementation conflates two distinct states: 1. User has Git configured but genuinely has no tasks 2. User has no Git providers configured (OSS mode) This creates a confusing experience where users don't understand they need to set up Git integrations to see suggested tasks. ## Current Behavior - User opens the home screen in OSS mode without any Git providers configured - The "Suggested Tasks" section header is displayed - Below it, only "No tasks available" text appears - No indication of what action the user should take ## Expected Behavior When no Git providers are configured (OSS mode), the component should: - Display a helpful message explaining that Git integration is required - Provide a button/link directing users to `/settings/integrations` - Use existing button components (e.g., `Button` or `LinkButton`) to maintain UI consistency ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] When `APP_MODE === "oss"` and `providers.length === 0`, display an informative empty state instead of "No tasks available" - [ ] The empty state should include explanatory text (e.g., "Connect a Git provider to see suggested tasks from your repositories") - [ ] The empty state should include a CTA button linking to `/settings/integrations` - [ ] The button should use existing button components from the codebase (check `frontend/src/components/shared/buttons/`) to maintain design consistency - [ ] Add appropriate i18n keys for the new text strings - [ ] The existing "No tasks available" message should only appear when the user HAS Git configured but has no actual tasks ## Relevant Files - [task-suggestions.tsx](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/components/features/home/tasks/task-suggestions.tsx) - Main component to modify - [use-suggested-tasks.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/query/use-suggested-tasks.ts) - Hook that fetches tasks - [use-should-show-user-features.ts](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/hooks/use-should-show-user-features.ts) - Contains the logic for detecting OSS mode + no providers - [frontend/src/components/shared/buttons/](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/tree/main/frontend/src/components/shared/buttons) - Existing button components to reuse - [translation.json](https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/blob/main/frontend/src/i18n/translation.json) - Add new i18n keys > [!NOTE] > This only affects OSS mode. In SaaS mode, users are always authenticated with Git providers, so this empty state would not appear.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12555.title: Add empty state guidance for Suggested Tasks when no Git provider is configured

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12564.body:
## Problem Statement

When working on a long task, the conversation history grows and can cause:
- Context window overflow leading to automatic condensation
- Slower responses as more context is processed
- Accumulated "stale" context that's no longer relevant

Currently, the only way to get a fresh start is to spawn a new conversation via `/new` or through the UI, which **restarts the entire runtime** (container creation, repository cloning, dependency installation, etc.). This is wasteful when:
- All relevant changes are already committed via git
- The runtime environment is correctly configured
- You just want a "clean slate" for conversation history, not the workspace

Ref: [slack discussion](https://openhands-ai.slack.com/archives/C06U8UTKSAD/p1769095721539569)

## Proposed Solution

Add a `/clear` command that:
1. **Clears all conversation history** from the agent's context
2. **Preserves the runtime state** - container, filesystem, installed packages, environment variables
3. Optionally provides a brief acknowledgment like "Conversation history cleared. Runtime state preserved."

### Usage
```
/clear
```

The agent would then start fresh from a "Hello, how can I help?" state but with access to the existing workspace and all its contents.

## Alternatives Considered

### 1. Enhanced `/condense` with aggressive mode
Extend the existing `/condense` command with flags like:
- `/condense --aggressive` - Reduce to minimal context (just system prompt + 1-line summary)
- `/condense --full` - Same as /clear but keeps a brief summary of accomplishments

**Pros:** Builds on existing infrastructure
**Cons:** Not a true clear; still carries some context

### 2. `/checkpoint` and `/restore` system
- `/checkpoint [name]` - Save current conversation state
- `/clear --since [checkpoint]` - Clear history since a checkpoint
- `/restore [checkpoint]` - Restore to a previous point

**Pros:** Granular control, reversible
**Cons:** More complex implementation, storage overhead

### 3. `/focus [topic]` command
A softer approach where the user signals a topic change, and the system automatically deprioritizes older context during summarization.

**Pros:** More natural workflow
**Cons:** Less explicit, may not fully solve the context pollution problem

## Relationship to Existing Features

| Command | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| `/condense` | Summarizes history but keeps context |
| `/new` | Fresh conversation, **restarts runtime** |
| `/clear` (proposed) | Clears history, **preserves runtime** |

## Use Cases

1. **Task completion pivot**: "I've finished setting up the database, committed the changes. Now I want to work on the frontend with a fresh context."

2. **Context pollution cleanup**: "The agent got confused by earlier failed approaches. I want to clear that context and try fresh."

3. **Session extension**: "My conversation is getting long but I'm not done. Clear the history so I can continue without hitting context limits."

## Additional Context

Related discussion from the community:
> "Is there a way to delete conversation history of a chat in OpenHands agent? I don't want to spawn a new runtime (container creation, repo cloning etc.). All the relevant changes I made in the current conversation is preserved through a git commit. Hence it's safe to delete the entire conversation history."

cc: @enyst (who worked on /condense)

---
### If you find this feature request useful, please add a 👍 to help prioritize!
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12564.title: Feature: `/new` command to reset conversation history while keeping runtime alive

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12572.body:
## Description

Users are experiencing a "Failed to resend verification email" error with HTTP status code 400 when clicking the "Resend verification" button on the email verification modal during the login flow.

## Error Message

- "Failed to resend verification email"
- "Request failed with status code 400"

## Root Cause

The `login.tsx` page does not pass the `userId` prop to `EmailVerificationModal`. When a user signs up with email:

1. Backend redirects to `/login?email_verification_required=true&user_id={user_id}`
2. `useEmailVerification()` hook extracts `userId` from URL params
3. **But** `login.tsx` does not destructure `userId` from the hook
4. `EmailVerificationModal` receives `userId: undefined`
5. When "Resend verification" is clicked, the API call lacks `user_id` in the body
6. Since the user is not authenticated (on login page), backend returns 400: `"user_id is required in request body or user must be authenticated"`

## Impact

This affects **all users** who:
- Sign up with email (not OAuth)
- Need to verify their email
- Click "Resend verification" from the login page

Datadog logs show 50+ occurrences from 20 unique users in the past 5 hours.

## Code Location

`frontend/src/routes/login.tsx`

## Expected Fix

1. Add `userId` to the destructured values from `useEmailVerification()`
2. Pass `userId` as a prop to `EmailVerificationModal`

```tsx
// Before
const {
  emailVerified,
  hasDuplicatedEmail,
  recaptchaBlocked,
  emailVerificationModalOpen,
  setEmailVerificationModalOpen,
} = useEmailVerification();

// After
const {
  emailVerified,
  hasDuplicatedEmail,
  recaptchaBlocked,
  emailVerificationModalOpen,
  setEmailVerificationModalOpen,
  userId,  // Add this
} = useEmailVerification();
```

And pass it to the modal:

```tsx
<EmailVerificationModal
  onClose={() => setEmailVerificationModalOpen(false)}
  userId={userId}  // Add this
/>
```
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12572.title: [Bug]: "Failed to resend verification email" (400 error) on login page

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12576.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug? (If one exists, thumbs up or comment on the issue instead).

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

## Problem

The `/download` API endpoint was returning a 500 error when attempting to export a conversation trajectory.

---

## Root Cause

In `live_status_app_conversation_service.py`, the `export_conversation` method was calling `page_iterator` with an incorrect parameter name.

**Before (incorrect):**
```python
async for event in page_iterator(
    self.event_service.search_events,
    conversation_id__eq=conversation_id
):
```

The search_events method expects conversation_id as its parameter name, not conversation_id__eq.
This caused a TypeError because the required positional argument was missing.

**Fix**

Changed the parameter name to match the method signature.

```python
async for event in page_iterator(
    self.event_service.search_events,
    conversation_id=conversation_id
):
```

### OpenHands Installation

Docker command in README

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

None

### Logs, Errors, Screenshots, and Additional Context

_No response_

Those Agent Skills should then be user invocable through a Slash command based on the `name` attribute in the frontmatter of that Agent Skill. - For example, an Agent Skill named `random-number` should be invocable as `/random-number` - If the Agent Skill has frontmatter `user-invocable: false` set, then the Agent Skill should NOT appear in the slash command menu - If the CLI is also started with a Plugin, any slash commands in that `commands/` directory should also be made available in the slash command menu.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12628.body:
## Problem Statement

Users currently cannot change or attach a repository to an existing conversation. Once a conversation is started (with or without a repository), the repository selection is permanently locked for that conversation's lifetime. This creates friction in several common workflows.

## User Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Starting Without Repository Access
**Context:** A user begins working on a public repository before granting repository access to OpenHands.

**Steps:**
1. User starts a conversation to work on a public GitHub repo without configuring OAuth/permissions
2. Agent successfully completes the work in the cloud environment
3. User decides to fork the repo and submit a PR
4. User grants OpenHands the necessary repository permissions
5. Agent attempts to push but encounters confusion about remote configuration
6. After troubleshooting, agent successfully pushes to the fork

**Pain Point:** User now wants to continue working in this conversation but needs to attach the forked repository. There is no way to do this - they must either:
- Manually manage git operations outside OpenHands
- Start a completely new conversation (losing context and history)
- Copy-paste previous context into a new conversation

### Scenario 2: Switching from Original to Fork
**Context:** A user starts work on an original repository but needs to switch to their fork for PR submission.

**Steps:**
1. User starts conversation with `original-owner/repo`
2. Agent makes changes
3. User realizes they need to push to `their-username/repo` (fork)
4. User wants to switch the conversation's repository to their fork

**Pain Point:** No ability to change the repository means starting over or manual workarounds.

### Scenario 3: Wrong Repository Selected
**Context:** A user accidentally selects the wrong repository when starting a conversation.

**Steps:**
1. User starts conversation with `repo-A`
2. User realizes they meant to work on `repo-B`
3. Early enough that no significant work has been done

**Pain Point:** Must delete and recreate conversation even if no work has occurred.

### Scenario 4: Repository Access Granted Mid-Conversation
**Context:** A user starts without any repository, then later wants to add one.

**Steps:**
1. User starts conversation for general coding questions/tasks
2. Conversation evolves into specific repository work
3. User grants repository access to OpenHands
4. User wants to attach repository to leverage branch management, PR features, etc.

**Pain Point:** Cannot enhance an existing conversation with repository features.

## Proposed Solution

Add the ability to **change/attach a repository to an existing conversation** through the UI and API.

### UI Changes

**Location:** Conversation settings or header area (where repository info is displayed)

**New Component:** "Change Repository" or "Attach Repository" button/dropdown

**User Flow:**
1. User clicks "Change Repository" button in active conversation
2. System shows repository selection interface (same as conversation creation)
3. User selects new repository and branch
4. System validates permissions
5. System updates conversation metadata
6. System informs agent of repository change via system message
7. Agent acknowledges the change and can now work with new repository

### API Changes

**New Endpoint or Extend Existing:**
```
PATCH /api/conversations/{conversation_id}
```

**Current Request Body:**
```json
{
  "title": "string"
}
```

**Proposed Enhanced Request Body:**
```json
{
  "title": "string",
  "selected_repository": "string | null",
  "selected_branch": "string | null", 
  "git_provider": "github | gitlab | bitbucket | azure_devops | forgejo | null"
}
```

**Validation Rules:**
- User must have appropriate permissions for new repository
- All fields are optional (only update what's provided)
- If `selected_repository` is provided, `git_provider` should also be provided
- `selected_branch` can be updated independently if repository stays the same
- Setting to `null` values removes repository association

### Agent Context Update

When repository is changed:
1. Send system message to agent: `"Repository changed from {old_repo} to {new_repo} on branch {branch}. Previous git remote configuration may need updating."`
2. Update agent's environment variables if applicable
3. Clear any cached repository metadata

## Scope & Implementation Considerations

### In Scope

#### Minimal MVP (Phase 1)
- ✅ Update conversation metadata (repository, branch, provider)
- ✅ UI button/interface for changing repository
- ✅ Permission validation before allowing change
- ✅ System message to agent about change
- ✅ Backend API endpoint enhancement
- ✅ Frontend repository selector integration
- ✅ Works for both V0 and V1 conversations

#### Full Feature (Phase 2)
- ✅ Automatic git remote update in workspace (if applicable)
- ✅ Handle repository history/audit trail (who changed what, when)
- ✅ Option to re-clone repository or keep existing workspace
- ✅ Smart suggestions (detect fork relationships)
- ✅ Bulk update for sub-conversations

### Out of Scope (Future Enhancements)

- ❌ Multi-repository support in single conversation (separate feature #12608)
- ❌ Automatic workspace migration between repositories
- ❌ Repository change history timeline/rollback
- ❌ Forking a conversation with repository change (covered by #8560, #12466)
- ❌ Changing repository in archived/completed conversations

### Technical Considerations

#### Database Schema
**Changes to conversation metadata:**
- `selected_repository`: Already exists, needs to be mutable
- `selected_branch`: Already exists, needs to be mutable
- `git_provider`: Already exists, needs to be mutable
- Add: `repository_changed_at` (timestamp, nullable)
- Add: `repository_change_count` (int, default 0) - for analytics

#### Runtime/Workspace Handling

**Option A: Keep Existing Workspace**
- Repository change only updates metadata
- Agent/user manually handles git remotes
- Simpler, lower risk
- Good for MVP

**Option B: Smart Workspace Update**
- Attempt to update git remote automatically
- If workspace is clean, offer to re-clone
- More complex, better UX
- Good for Phase 2

**Recommendation:** Start with Option A for MVP, add Option B in Phase 2

#### Security & Permissions
- Verify user has access to new repository before allowing change
- Check repository visibility (public vs private)
- Validate OAuth scopes are sufficient
- Log repository changes for audit purposes

#### Conversation State Considerations
- **Active conversations:** Allow changes, warn if agent is currently running
- **Paused conversations:** Allow changes freely
- **Completed conversations:** Consider blocking or requiring explicit unlock
- **Archived conversations:** Block repository changes

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Agent is actively running:**
   - Show warning: "Agent is currently active. Changing repository may cause confusion. Continue?"
   - Consider pausing agent first

2. **Uncommitted changes exist:**
   - Warn user about uncommitted changes in workspace
   - Suggest committing or stashing first
   - Option: "Commit current changes before switching"

3. **Repository becomes inaccessible:**
   - Original repository deleted or permissions revoked
   - Allow changing to accessible repository
   - Handle gracefully with clear error messages

4. **Branch doesn't exist in new repository:**
   - Validate branch exists before allowing change
   - Offer to change to default branch
   - Show available branches

5. **Switching between providers:**
   - GitHub → GitLab, etc.
   - Ensure token is available for new provider
   - Update git remote URL format appropriately

6. **Removing repository (setting to null):**
   - Allow conversations to become repository-less
   - Disable git-related UI features
   - Agent can still access workspace files

## Acceptance Criteria

### Must Have (MVP)
- [ ] User can click "Change Repository" button in conversation UI
- [ ] Repository selector appears with user's accessible repositories
- [ ] User can select new repository and branch
- [ ] System validates user has permission to access selected repository
- [ ] Conversation metadata updates successfully
- [ ] Agent receives system message about repository change
- [ ] Updated repository info displays in conversation header
- [ ] API endpoint accepts repository change requests
- [ ] Works for conversations started without a repository
- [ ] Works for conversations started with a different repository
- [ ] Appropriate error messages for permission/validation failures

### Should Have (Phase 2)
- [ ] Warning when agent is actively running
- [ ] Detection of uncommitted changes
- [ ] Automatic git remote update in workspace
- [ ] Audit log of repository changes
- [ ] Repository change count visible to admins
- [ ] Smart fork detection and suggestions
- [ ] Branch validation with helpful errors
- [ ] Option to re-clone repository after change

### Nice to Have (Future)
- [ ] Bulk repository change for sub-conversations
- [ ] Repository change history timeline in UI
- [ ] Undo last repository change
- [ ] Suggested repositories based on conversation content
- [ ] Cross-provider repository migration assistance

## Alternative Approaches Considered

### Alternative 1: Clone Conversation Feature
**Approach:** Instead of changing repository, create a new conversation with different repository.

**Pros:**
- Preserves original conversation intact
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Already being considered in #12466

**Cons:**
- Loses the context continuity
- User has to manage multiple conversations
- Doesn't solve the "I picked wrong repo" scenario

**Verdict:** Complementary feature, not a replacement

### Alternative 2: Multi-Repository Support
**Approach:** Allow multiple repositories per conversation.

**Pros:**
- More powerful and flexible
- Handles complex scenarios
- Already proposed in #12608

**Cons:**
- Much more complex to implement
- Different use case than repository switching
- Overkill for simple scenarios

**Verdict:** Different feature, addresses different need

### Alternative 3: Repository-less by Default
**Approach:** All conversations start without repositories, add as needed.

**Pros:**
- Maximum flexibility
- Aligns with progressive disclosure

**Cons:**
- Changes core UX significantly
- Doesn't align with current workflow
- Requires major refactoring

**Verdict:** Too large a change, incompatible with current model

## Questions for Discussion

1. **Workspace Handling:** Should we automatically update git remotes in the workspace, or leave it manual for MVP?

2. **State Restrictions:** Should we allow repository changes for completed/archived conversations?

3. **Conversation History:** Should repository changes be visible in the conversation timeline/history?

4. **Bulk Operations:** If a conversation has sub-conversations, should repository change propagate?

5. **Undo Feature:** Should we allow undoing a repository change (within a time window)?

6. **Analytics:** What events should we track for repository changes?

## Related Issues

- #12466 - [PRD] MVP - Clone a Conversation
- #8560 - [PRD] Fork a Conversation  
- #12608 - feat(microagent): add dependency_repos frontmatter field for multiple repos

## Impact Assessment

**User Value:** High
- Reduces friction in common workflows
- Prevents loss of conversation context
- Enables repository permission fixes mid-conversation

**Implementation Complexity:** Medium
- Backend: Moderate (API update, validation)
- Frontend: Moderate (UI component, state management)
- Agent: Low (system message handling)

**Risk Level:** Low-Medium
- Well-contained feature
- Existing metadata updates proven
- Can be feature-flagged for gradual rollout

**Estimated Effort:** 
- MVP (Phase 1): 2-3 sprints
- Full Feature (Phase 2): Additional 1-2 sprints

---

_This issue was created based on real user feedback. Original user scenario: User started working on a public repo without granting repo access, agent solved the issue, user forked the repo and set up permissions, agent pushed successfully, but now the user cannot attach the forked repo to continue the conversation with proper integration._
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12628.title: [Feature Request] Allow attaching/changing repository for existing conversations

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12696.body:
## Summary

Add a new `/launch` UI endpoint that displays a modal for starting conversations with pre-configured plugins. This enables integration with the external plugin catalog - when a user clicks "Launch" on a plugin in the catalog, they'll be directed to this endpoint which shows a confirmation modal with parameter input fields before starting the conversation.

**Parent issue:** [APP-628](https://linear.app/all-hands-ai/issue/APP-628/plugin-marketplace-gui)

## Background

This builds on OpenHands/OpenHands#12338 which added backend support for starting conversations with remote plugins via the REST API. The plugin catalog is a separate application that needs a way to deep-link into OpenHands to launch conversations with specific plugins loaded.

## User Flow

1. User browses the plugin catalog (external app)
2. User clicks "Launch" on a plugin
3. User is redirected to OpenHands `/launch?plugins=...&message=...`
4. If not authenticated → redirect to login, then return to `/launch` with params preserved
5. Modal appears showing:
   * Display message (from catalog, describing what will happen)
   * Parameter input fields grouped by plugin (for plugins that require configuration)
   * Collapsible section listing any additional plugins without parameters
   * "Start Conversation" button
   * X close button in top-right corner
6. User fills in any required parameters
7. User clicks "Start Conversation" → conversation created with plugins + parameters → navigate to conversation
8. User clicks X → navigate back to referrer (plugin catalog)

## Query Parameters

**Option 1: Base64 encoded JSON (production use, supports multiple plugins)**

```
/launch?plugins=<base64-encoded-json>&message=<url-encoded-text>
```

Where `plugins` decodes to:

```json
[
  {
    "source": "github:owner/repo",
    "ref": "v1.0.0",
    "repo_path": "plugins/my-plugin",
    "parameters": {
      "timeout": 30,
      "region": "",
      "debug": false
    }
  }
]
```

Parameters represent the fields to display with optional default values. Input type is inferred from value type:

* `number` → number input
* `boolean` → checkbox
* `string` → text input (default)

**Option 2: Simple params (dev/testing only, single plugin)**

```
/launch?plugin_source=github:owner/repo&plugin_ref=v1.0.0&message=Launch+My+Plugin
```

Note: Simple params option is for development/debugging and may be removed later.

## Modal UI

### Scenario A: Plugins with parameters + plugins without

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                              [X]    │
│                                                     │
│   Launch AWS Deployment Plugin                      │
│                                                     │
│   ▾ aws-deploy                                      │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  region      [us-west-2___]                 │   │
│   │  timeout     [30__________]                 │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                     │
│   ▸ Additional Plugins                              │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  • github:owner/logging-plugin @ v1.0       │   │
│   │  • github:owner/metrics-plugin              │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                     │
│              [ Start Conversation ]                 │
│                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Scenario B: Only plugins without parameters

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                              [X]    │
│                                                     │
│   Launch Code Review Plugin                         │
│                                                     │
│   ▸ Plugins                                         │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  • github:owner/code-review @ v2.1          │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                     │
│              [ Start Conversation ]                 │
│                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Scenario C: Only plugins with parameters (multiple)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                              [X]    │
│                                                     │
│   Launch Data Pipeline                              │
│                                                     │
│   ▾ data-pipeline                                   │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  source_bucket  [______________]            │   │
│   │  batch_size     [100___________]            │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                     │
│   ▾ transformer                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │  format        [json_________]              │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                     │
│              [ Start Conversation ]                 │
│                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Display Logic

* If plugin has parameters → show as expandable section with form inputs (expanded by default)
* If plugin has no parameters → group into "Additional Plugins" or "Plugins" collapsible list
* If no plugins have parameters → just show "Plugins" collapsible list
* Display message always shown at top

## Technical Implementation

1. **New route** in `src/routes.ts`: `/launch` → `routes/launch.tsx`
2. **New component(s)**:
   * `src/routes/launch.tsx` - route handler
   * `src/components/features/launch/plugin-launch-modal.tsx` - modal component
   * Parse query params (`plugins` base64 JSON or simple params)
   * Sanitize display message (XSS prevention)
   * Render modal with plugin parameter forms
   * Handle auth redirect (leverage existing `returnTo` pattern)
3. **Update frontend types** in `v1-conversation-service.types.ts`:

   ```typescript
   interface PluginSpec {
     source: string;
     ref?: string | null;
     repo_path?: string | null;
     parameters?: Record<string, unknown> | null;
   }
   
   interface V1AppConversationStartRequest {
     // ... existing fields
     plugins?: PluginSpec[] | null;
   }
   ```
4. **Update** `useCreateConversation` hook to accept `plugins` parameter
5. **Update** `V1ConversationService.createConversation()` to pass plugins to API

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/launch` route exists and is protected by authentication
- [ ] Unauthenticated users are redirected to login, then back to `/launch` with params preserved
- [ ] Modal displays sanitized message from `message` query param
- [ ] Plugins with parameters show expandable sections with form inputs (number, text, checkbox based on type)
- [ ] Plugins without parameters show in collapsible "Additional Plugins" or "Plugins" list
- [ ] Form inputs are pre-filled with default values when provided
- [ ] "Start Conversation" button creates conversation with plugins + user-entered parameters
- [ ] X button navigates back to referrer
- [ ] Both base64 JSON and simple params formats are supported
- [ ] Invalid/malformed params show appropriate error handling
- [ ] Works correctly when no plugins have parameters
- [ ] Works correctly with single plugin
- [ ] Works correctly with multiple plugins

## Security Considerations

* Display message must be sanitized to prevent XSS
* Parameter values should be sanitized before display
* Plugin parameters are passed to backend which has its own validation (path traversal protection, etc.)

## Related

* [APP-628](https://linear.app/all-hands-ai/issue/APP-628/plugin-marketplace-gui) - Plugin Marketplace GUI (parent)
* OpenHands/OpenHands#12338 - Backend: Start conversations with remote plugins via REST API
* [APP-630](https://linear.app/all-hands-ai/issue/APP-630/prd-openhands-plugins) - \[PRD\] OpenHands Plugins
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12696.title: feat(frontend): Add /launch route for starting conversations with plugins from external catalog

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12704.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug?

- [x] I have checked the existing issues.

### Describe the bug and reproduction steps

When using the OpenHands UI with a long V1 conversation, resizing the browser window across the mobile/desktop breakpoint (1024px) triggers a reload that can take **30 seconds to over a minute** to complete. During this time, the conversation displays a skeleton loader.

**Reproduction Steps:**

1. Start a V1 conversation and interact with it until there are many messages (the longer the conversation, the more noticeable the issue)
2. Wait at least 30 seconds after the last interaction
3. Resize the browser window to cross the 1024px width threshold (e.g., from 1200px to 900px, or vice versa)
4. Observe that a skeleton loader appears and the conversation takes a very long time to reload

**Expected Behavior:**

Resizing the window should be instantaneous or near-instantaneous. The conversation history is already loaded and should not need to be refetched.

**Actual Behavior:**

The UI shows a loading skeleton for 30+ seconds while it appears to refetch and reprocess the entire conversation history.

### OpenHands Version

1.2.x (V1 conversations)

### Operating System

All (observed on macOS, but likely affects all platforms)

### Additional Context

- The issue is proportional to conversation length - short conversations may not show noticeable delay
- The issue only manifests after ~30 seconds of inactivity in the conversation
- This affects V1 conversations specifically
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12704.title: [Bug]: Resizing browser window triggers slow reload in long V1 conversations

AgentErrorEvent currently triggers a toast in addition to the inline chat error message.

And even when the conversation page is reloaded and historical events are loaded, the toast appears again even for old errors, sometimes with very large payloads.

2. Observe the toast appearing with a large content, blocking visibility of the tab.
3. Reload the conversation page.
4. Observe the toast reappearing as past events are loaded.

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12832.body:
## Bug Description

When PRs are created via the MCP tools without a valid conversation ID, the PR description includes a broken link that points to `https://app.all-hands.dev/conversations/None` instead of omitting the link entirely.

## Examples

Found 5+ PRs with this broken link pattern:

- [OpenHands/benchmarks#410](https://github.com/OpenHands/benchmarks/pull/410) - Example from benchmarks repo
- [OpenHands/OpenHands#12442](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/12442) - fix: Extract HTTP fields in uvicorn access JSON logs
- [OpenHands/OpenHands#12264](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/12264) - APP-307 Add Google Cloud Storage-based EventService implementation
- [OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#1733](https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/1733) - fix: Add JSON formatter for uvicorn access logs
- [OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#712](https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/712) - Implement Agent Delegation Feature

Search query to find affected PRs: [`org:OpenHands is:pr "continue refining the PR" "conversations/None"`](https://github.com/search?q=org%3AOpenHands+is%3Apr+%22continue+refining+the+PR%22+%22conversations%2FNone%22&type=pullrequests)

## Root Cause

In `openhands/server/routes/mcp.py`, the `get_conversation_link` function doesn't check if `conversation_id` is `None` before formatting the URL:

```python
async def get_conversation_link(
    service: GitService, conversation_id: str, body: str
) -> str:
    # ...
    conversation_url = CONVERSATION_URL.format(conversation_id)  # This becomes /conversations/None
```

The `conversation_id` is obtained from the `X-OpenHands-ServerConversation-ID` header, which can be `None` when:
1. The PR is created outside of an OpenHands conversation context
2. The header is missing from the request

## Proposed Fix

Add a check for `None` or empty `conversation_id` to skip adding the link:

```python
async def get_conversation_link(
    service: GitService, conversation_id: str | None, body: str
) -> str:
    if server_config.app_mode != AppMode.SAAS:
        return body

    # Don't add a broken link if conversation_id is None or empty
    if not conversation_id:
        return body

    # ... rest of the function
```

## Impact

- User experience: Clicking the broken link leads to a dead page
- Trust: Makes the GitHub integration appear buggy
- Confusion: Users may report issues or waste time investigating
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12832.title: Bug: Broken 'continue refining the PR' links with conversations/None in PR descriptions

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12846.body:
## Description

When using `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` as the model, the resolver fails immediately with:

```
litellm.exceptions.BadRequestError: AnthropicException - {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"`temperature` and `top_p` cannot both be specified for this model. Please use only one."},"request_id":"..."}
```

The error occurs on the very first LLM call in `agent_controller.py`, and also in `guess_success` in `issue_definitions.py`.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure OpenHands resolver with `LLM_MODEL=anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`
2. Run `python -m openhands.resolver.resolve_issue`
3. Fails immediately with the error above

## Environment

- openhands-ai 1.3.0
- LiteLLM (whatever version is pulled in by openhands-ai 1.3.0)
- Direct Anthropic API (not Bedrock)

## Notes

- Other Claude models (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`) work fine
- `LITELLM_DROP_PARAMS=true` does not help — both `temperature` and `top_p` are individually valid Anthropic params, they just can't be sent together for this model
- A similar issue (#11248) was fixed for Bedrock-prefixed model names, but the fix doesn't cover direct Anthropic API model names like `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12846.title: claude-opus-4-6 fails: temperature and top_p cannot both be specified

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12865.body:
## Description

Users report seeing "Unknown event" displayed in the UI when the agent is about to read a file (e.g., before "Reading README.md" is shown). After the file read operation completes, there is another observation which shows correctly (e.g., "Read README.md").

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a conversation with the agent
2. Ask the agent to read or view a file (triggers a `FileEditorAction` with `command: "view"`)
3. Observe the chat interface during the action execution

**Expected Behavior:** The UI should show maybe only "Reading <path>" while the action is being executed. It shouldn't show "Unknown event".

**Actual Behavior:** "Unknown event" is shown. Then another event "Read <path>" appears. The second has the correct observation message.

## Technical Investigation

The "Unknown event" message originates from `EVENT$UNKNOWN_EVENT` i18n key (`frontend/src/i18n/translation.json`).

In `frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/get-event-content.tsx`, the title defaults to "Unknown event" when:
```typescript
return {
  title: title || i18n.t("EVENT$UNKNOWN_EVENT"),  // line 210
  details: details || i18n.t("EVENT$UNKNOWN_EVENT"),
};
```

This happens when `title` is an empty string, which occurs when an event fails the `isActionEvent()` type guard in `getActionEventTitle()` (line 43-44).

### Relevant Type Guard

The `isActionEvent` type guard in `frontend/src/types/v1/type-guards.ts` requires:
- `event.source === "agent"`
- `"action" in event && event.action !== null`
- `typeof event.action === "object" && "kind" in event.action`
- `"tool_name" in event && "tool_call_id" in event`
- Both `tool_name` and `tool_call_id` must be strings

### Potential Causes

1. **Event arrives with missing fields:** The SDK's `ActionEvent` has `action: Action | None = Field(default=None, ...)`, meaning `action` can be `null`. If an event arrives with `action: null`, it would fail `isActionEvent()`.

2. **Streaming/partial events:** Events might be arriving before all fields are populated during WebSocket streaming. (HUMAN: ? this is LLM written)

3. **Event timing:** There might be a brief window where an event is rendered before it's fully validated.

### Related Files

- `frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-content-helpers/get-event-content.tsx`
- `frontend/src/types/v1/type-guards.ts`
- `frontend/src/components/v1/chat/event-message.tsx`
- `frontend/src/stores/use-event-store.ts`

## Environment

- OpenHands Cloud
- V1 event system

## Additional Context

The issue appears to be UI/UX only and doesn't affect actual behavior - the file read operations complete successfully. However, it's disconcerting from a user perspective.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12865.title: "Unknown event" shown before file read operations complete

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12984.body:
HUMAN: this one was funny, I thought OpenAI did again something weird and my GPT-5.2 got confused. Links below 🫠

TL;DR: GH comments and GH instructions directly in the system prompt lead to tales to the unexpected. 😅

—-

### Problem

In the V1 resolver flow (app_server / SDK conversations), resolver context (e.g. PR/MR/issue metadata, previous comments, and handling instructions) has been passed through `system_message_suffix`. That effectively elevates integration context to **system prompt priority**, which is higher priority than intended.

This differs from V0 where resolver instructions are injected as lower-priority user-role context (workspace recall).

### Why this breaks expectations

- System prompt should remain reserved for core safety/behavior constraints.
- Integration context like “you are checked out on branch X”, “previous comments”, etc. should be **lower priority** than the base system prompt.
- Different models/providers can treat system content differently; putting lots of contextual text in system can cause unexpected behavior / prompt conflicts.
- The LLM refuses to post a comment when the user asks, because it has been told _“Do NOT post a comment”_:
    - https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/2090#issuecomment-3939031597
    - https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/2166#issuecomment-3939347252
    - [Slack discussion](https://openhands-ai.slack.com/archives/C07V9BMGHH6/p1771704532793949)

### Expected behavior

For V1 resolver conversations, integration context should be placed in the **initial user message** (or another lower-priority channel), not `system_message_suffix`.

### Proposed fix

- Set `system_message_suffix = None` for resolver flows.
- Render a dedicated initial user message template that includes:
  - general context (branch/PR/issue, etc.)
  - the triggering comment
  - previous comments / thread context

### Related PR

A PR is in progress to implement this for the GitHub V1 resolver.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#12984.title: V1 resolver should not inject PR/issue context via system_message_suffix

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13005.body:
## Description

When using the Changes tab in the Web UI for a V1 conversation with a GitLab repository that has a nested group path (e.g., `modernhealth/frontend-guild/pan`), the tab fails to load and shows a 500 error in the browser console.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a new conversation with a GitLab repository that has a nested path structure (e.g., `group/subgroup/repo`)
2. Let the agent make some changes to files
3. Click on the Changes tab in the conversation UI

## Expected Behavior

The Changes tab should display the list of modified files.

## Actual Behavior

The Changes tab fails to load and the browser console shows a 500 error with the message:

```
{
  "detail": "Internal Server Error",
  "exception": "Directory does not exist: /workspace/project/frontend-guild"
}
```

Note: The error shows `frontend-guild` (the middle group name) instead of `pan` (the actual repository name).

## Environment

- OpenHands Enterprise Server (latest)
- V1 conversations
- GitLab as the git provider
- Repository path format: `group/subgroup/repository`

## Additional Context

This issue only affects repositories with nested paths (3+ segments). Standard `owner/repo` format works correctly.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13005.title: Changes tab shows 500 error for GitLab repositories with nested group paths

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13138.body:
## Context

Type errors in the enterprise folder are not being caught by mypy. This allowed a bug where `str | None` was passed to a function expecting `str`, causing production errors.

**Example**: #13135 - A `ValidationError` occurred because `keycloak_user_id` (which can be `None`) was passed to `UserData` which requires a `str`.

## Root Cause

Enterprise code uses bare imports like `from integrations.github.github_view import GithubFactory` (without `enterprise.` prefix). Mypy cannot resolve these imports without proper path configuration.

Current behavior:
1. `mypy.ini` has `ignore_missing_imports = True`
2. Unresolved imports become `Any` type
3. `Any` is compatible with everything → type mismatches not caught

## Proposed Fix (Verified)

**1. Update `enterprise/dev_config/python/mypy.ini`:**
```ini
# Remove explicit_package_bases = True (causes dual module name conflict)
```

**2. Update `enterprise/dev_config/python/.pre-commit-config.yaml`:**
```yaml
# Use -p (package) to avoid dual module name conflict when using MYPYPATH
# MYPYPATH=enterprise allows resolving bare imports like "from integrations.xxx"
# Note: tests package excluded to avoid dual module name conflict with core openhands tests
entry: bash -c 'export MYPYPATH=enterprise && mypy --config-file enterprise/dev_config/python/mypy.ini -p integrations -p server -p storage -p sync -p experiments\n```
```

## Pre-existing Type Errors

This fix exposes **255 pre-existing type errors** across 43 files (production code only, tests excluded). These should be addressed incrementally.

## Verification

After applying the fix, running `poetry run pre-commit run mypy --all-files --config ./dev_config/python/.pre-commit-config.yaml` correctly catches the original bug:

```
enterprise/integrations/github/github_manager.py:186: error: Argument 2 to "create_github_view_from_payload" of "GithubFactory" has incompatible type "str | None"; expected "str"  [arg-type]
```
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13138.title: Fix mypy type checking for enterprise folder

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13201.body:
## Description
GLM-4.7 is currently not available in the frontend model selector, while other models like GPT-5.2, Gemini-3-Flash, and Kimi-K2.5 are supported.

## Current Behavior
GLM-4.7 model is missing from the `OPENHANDS_MODELS` list in `openhands/utils/llm.py`, which means it doesn't appear in the frontend model selection dropdown.

## Expected Behavior
GLM-4.7 should be available in the frontend model selector alongside other supported models.

## Solution
Add `'openhands/glm-4.7'` to the `OPENHANDS_MODELS` list in `openhands/utils/llm.py`.

## Related Files
- `openhands/utils/llm.py` - Contains the hardcoded model lists for self-hosted mode

## Description
GLM-5 model exists in backend but is not available in the frontend dropdown selector.

## Current State
- Backend: GLM-5 is not listed in OPENHANDS_MODELS
- Frontend: GLM-5 is not in VERIFIED_MODELS or VERIFIED_OPENHANDS_MODELS arrays
- Model features: GLM-5 capabilities not defined

## Expected State
- GLM-5 should appear in frontend model selector
- Model should have proper capability definitions (function calling, reasoning, caching)

## Evidence
LiteLLM configuration confirms GLM-5 supports:
- Function calling
- Reasoning effort
- Prompt caching
- Tool choice

See: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/blob/main/model_prices_and_context_window.json

## Solution
Add GLM-5 to:
1. Backend OPENHANDS_MODELS list
2. Frontend VERIFIED_MODELS array
3. Frontend VERIFIED_OPENHANDS_MODELS array
4. Model capability patterns (function calling, reasoning, caching)

    -p 3000:3000 \
    --add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway \

llm_base_url changes to "https://api.openai.com"

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13268.body:
## Problem

When using the SDK with OpenHands Cloud, there are two ways to create a conversation — and **neither gives you both SaaS credentials AND agent customization**:

| | SaaS credentials (GitHub App token, LLM key) | Custom agent (tools, prompt, skills) | Visible in Cloud UI |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Path 1**: `POST /api/v1/app-conversations` | ✅ | ❌ (hardcoded default agent) | ✅ |
| **Path 2**: `OpenHandsCloudWorkspace` + SDK `Conversation` | ❌ (must supply your own) | ✅ | ❌ (see #13272) |

We need a way for SDK-created conversations to inherit the user's SaaS settings (credentials, repo context) while retaining full control over the agent.

## Minimal Reproduction

### Script A: SaaS credentials ✅, custom agent ❌

Using the `app-conversations` API gives you Cloud credentials and visibility, but you're stuck with the platform's default agent — no way to pass custom tools, system prompt, or skills.

```python
"""Path 1: app-conversations API — gets SaaS credentials, loses agent customization.

Requires: OPENHANDS_API_KEY
"""
import os
import httpx

CLOUD_URL = "https://app.all-hands.dev"
API_KEY = os.environ["OPENHANDS_API_KEY"]

with httpx.Client(timeout=60) as client:
    resp = client.post(
        f"{CLOUD_URL}/api/v1/app-conversations",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {API_KEY}"},
        json={
            "selected_repository": "OpenHands/OpenHands",
            "git_provider": "github",
            "initial_message": {"message": "Say hello"},
            # ❌ No way to specify:
            #   - custom tools
            #   - custom system prompt
            #   - custom skills / agent context
            #   - CLI mode vs browser mode
            # The platform builds a hardcoded default agent internally.
        },
    )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    task = resp.json()
    print(f"Conversation: {task.get('conversation_id')}")
    print("✅ Uses Cloud's GitHub App token, LLM key, repo cloned")
    print("✅ Visible in Cloud dashboard")
    print("❌ Cannot customize agent (tools, prompt, skills)")
```

### Script B: Custom agent ✅, SaaS credentials ❌

Following the [official SDK example](https://docs.openhands.dev/sdk/guides/agent-server/cloud-workspace#openhands-cloud-workspace), you get full agent control but lose all SaaS integration.

```python
"""Path 2: OpenHandsCloudWorkspace — full agent control, no SaaS credentials.

Requires: OPENHANDS_API_KEY, LLM_API_KEY

Follows: https://docs.openhands.dev/sdk/guides/agent-server/cloud-workspace
"""
import os
from pydantic import SecretStr
from openhands.sdk import LLM, Conversation
from openhands.tools.preset.default import get_default_agent
from openhands.workspace import OpenHandsCloudWorkspace

# ❌ Must provide your own LLM key — Cloud's key is not available
llm = LLM(
    model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
    api_key=SecretStr(os.environ["LLM_API_KEY"]),
)

# ✅ Full control over agent configuration
agent = get_default_agent(llm=llm, cli_mode=True)

with OpenHandsCloudWorkspace(
    cloud_api_url="https://app.all-hands.dev",
    cloud_api_key=os.environ["OPENHANDS_API_KEY"],
) as workspace:
    # ❌ Sandbox has no repo cloned, no GitHub App token, no setup scripts run
    conversation = Conversation(agent=agent, workspace=workspace)
    conversation.send_message("Say hello")
    conversation.run()
    # ❌ Conversation is invisible in Cloud dashboard (see #13272)
```

## Desired Outcome

A single path where SDK users can:
1. **Inherit SaaS credentials** — the sandbox gets the user's GitHub App token, LLM key, and provider tokens from their Cloud account
2. **Inherit repo context** — a specified repository is cloned with setup scripts executed
3. **Customize the agent** — full SDK control over tools, system prompt, skills, and plugins
4. **Be visible in Cloud UI** — conversations appear in the dashboard with full observability

## Relationship to #13272

This is a **follow-up to #13272** (conversations invisible in Cloud UI). The solution to #13272 will determine how conversations get registered with the Cloud, which directly informs how we solve credential/settings inheritance here. For example:

- If #13272 is solved by **auto-registering** every conversation created on a Cloud-provisioned sandbox, then this issue becomes about injecting SaaS credentials into those sandboxes (either automatically or via an explicit SDK call).
- If #13272 is solved by providing an **explicit registration API**, then we could extend that same API to also pull in the user's SaaS settings at registration time.

Either way, the architecture chosen in #13272 shapes the solution here.

## Current Workaround

SDK cloud mode requires users to provide their own `LLM_API_KEY` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`. Reviews are posted under the token owner's identity (not the Cloud bot), and conversations may not appear in the Cloud dashboard.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13268.title: feat: SDK-created conversations should inherit SaaS settings (credentials, repo context)

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13272.body:
## Summary

When using the SDK's `OpenHandsCloudWorkspace` to create a sandbox via `POST /api/v1/sandboxes` and then creating a conversation on the sandbox's agent-server, the conversation does not appear in the Cloud UI at `app.all-hands.dev`. The Cloud platform has no record of it — only the raw sandbox exists.

This means users cannot:

* Monitor the agent's progress in the Cloud UI
* Share or revisit conversation URLs
* Get Cloud-side observability (metrics, cost tracking, etc.)

## Root Cause

`OpenHandsCloudWorkspace` creates sandboxes via `POST /api/v1/sandboxes`, which provisions a raw agent-server container. The SDK's `Conversation` class then creates a conversation directly on the agent-server via `POST {agent_server}/api/conversations`.

The Cloud platform only tracks "app conversations" created through `POST /api/v1/app-conversations`. Since the raw sandbox flow bypasses this endpoint, the Cloud has no record of the conversation.

## Minimal Reproduction

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Minimal reproduction: conversations created via OpenHandsCloudWorkspace
are invisible in the Cloud UI.

Requires:
    OPENHANDS_API_KEY  - OpenHands Cloud API key
    LLM_API_KEY        - LLM API key
    LLM_BASE_URL       - (optional) LLM base URL
"""

import os
import httpx
from openhands.sdk import LLM, Agent, Conversation
from openhands.tools.preset.default import get_default_tools
from openhands.workspace import OpenHandsCloudWorkspace

CLOUD_URL = "https://app.all-hands.dev"
OPENHANDS_API_KEY = os.environ["OPENHANDS_API_KEY"]

# Step 1: Create sandbox via OpenHandsCloudWorkspace
print("Step 1: Creating sandbox via OpenHandsCloudWorkspace...")
workspace = OpenHandsCloudWorkspace(
    cloud_api_url=CLOUD_URL,
    cloud_api_key=OPENHANDS_API_KEY,
    keep_alive=True,
)
sandbox_id = workspace._sandbox_id
print(f"  Sandbox created: {sandbox_id}")
print(f"  Agent server: {workspace.host}")

# Step 2: Create a conversation on the sandbox
print("\nStep 2: Creating conversation on the sandbox's agent-server...")
llm_kwargs: dict = {
    "model": os.getenv("LLM_MODEL", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"),
    "usage_id": "repro",
    "drop_params": True,
}
if os.getenv("LLM_API_KEY"):
    llm_kwargs["api_key"] = os.environ["LLM_API_KEY"]
if os.getenv("LLM_BASE_URL"):
    llm_kwargs["base_url"] = os.environ["LLM_BASE_URL"]

llm = LLM(**llm_kwargs)
agent = Agent(
    llm=llm,
    tools=get_default_tools(enable_browser=False),
    system_prompt_kwargs={"cli_mode": True},
)
conversation = Conversation(agent=agent, workspace=workspace)
conv_id = str(conversation.state.id)
print(f"  Conversation created: {conv_id}")

# Step 3: Send a message and run
print("\nStep 3: Sending message and running agent...")
conversation.send_message("Say 'hello world' and nothing else.")
conversation.run(blocking=False)
print("  Agent triggered (non-blocking).")

# Step 4: Query Cloud API to show conversation is invisible
print("\nStep 4: Checking if conversation appears in Cloud UI...")
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {OPENHANDS_API_KEY}"}

with httpx.Client(timeout=15) as client:
    resp = client.get(
        f"{CLOUD_URL}/api/v1/app-conversations/search",
        headers=headers,
        params={"limit": 10},
    )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    page = resp.json()

recent_ids = [c["id"] for c in page.get("items", [])]
print(f"  Recent app-conversations (last 10): {recent_ids}")
print(f"  Our conversation ID: {conv_id}")

found = any(conv_id in cid for cid in recent_ids)
if found:
    print("\n✅ Conversation IS visible in Cloud UI.")
else:
    print("\n❌ Conversation is NOT visible in Cloud UI.")
    print("   The sandbox exists but the conversation is only on the")
    print("   agent-server inside the sandbox — the Cloud has no record of it.")

# Verify the sandbox itself exists
with httpx.Client(timeout=15) as client:
    resp = client.get(
        f"{CLOUD_URL}/api/v1/sandboxes",
        headers=headers,
        params={"id": sandbox_id},
    )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    sandboxes = resp.json()

if sandboxes and sandboxes[0]:
    sb = sandboxes[0]
    print(f"\n  Sandbox {sandbox_id} exists (status: {sb['status']})")
else:
    print(f"\n  Sandbox {sandbox_id} not found")
```

## Actual Output

```
Step 1: Creating sandbox via OpenHandsCloudWorkspace...
  Sandbox created: 5z7uzZmgTffzrWVU554Vrz
  Agent server: https://uelvydafsiusgrqo.prod-runtime.all-hands.dev

Step 2: Creating conversation on the sandbox's agent-server...
  Conversation created: 677f76d2-f25d-4b81-9c26-dbf26d5fdce3

Step 3: Sending message and running agent...
  Agent triggered (non-blocking).

Step 4: Checking if conversation appears in Cloud UI...
  Recent app-conversations (last 10): ['68f0eb27...', 'a5a7804f...', ...]
  Our conversation ID: 677f76d2-f25d-4b81-9c26-dbf26d5fdce3

❌ Conversation is NOT visible in Cloud UI.
   The sandbox exists but the conversation is only on the
   agent-server inside the sandbox — the Cloud has no record of it.

  Sandbox 5z7uzZmgTffzrWVU554Vrz exists (status: RUNNING)
```

## Expected Behavior

Conversations created on Cloud-provisioned sandboxes should be visible in the Cloud UI, either by:

1. **Registering conversations automatically**: When a conversation is created on a Cloud-provisioned sandbox's agent-server, the Cloud should detect and register it as an app-conversation.
2. **Or providing a registration API**: Expose an endpoint like `POST /api/v1/app-conversations/register` that accepts a `sandbox_id` + `conversation_id` to retroactively register an agent-server conversation with the Cloud.

## Impact

This affects all SDK users of `OpenHandsCloudWorkspace`. Conversations run on Cloud infrastructure but are invisible, untrackable, and non-shareable.

## Related

* [APP-924](https://linear.app/all-hands-ai/issue/APP-924/feat-sdk-created-conversations-should-inherit-saas-settings) — Broader issue about sandbox API lacking repository context support
* [software-agent-sdk PR #2341](<https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/2341>) — Where this was discovered during cloud mode implementation
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13272.title: [Bug] Conversations created via OpenHandsCloudWorkspace (POST /api/v1/sandboxes) are invisible in Cloud UI

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13280.body:
## Problem

When typing a message in the chat interface, the message content can be lost under certain conditions, requiring the user to retype their message.

## Scenarios Where Messages Are Lost

### 1. Background Refresh / Component Remount While Typing (Critical)
While typing a message, various background operations can cause the chat component to remount or the page to refresh, losing all typed content **before the user even presses send**.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Start typing a long message in the chat input
2. Wait for or trigger a background operation (conversation status change, runtime state change, query invalidation, etc.)
3. The text area is cleared and all typed content is lost

**Why this happens:** The typed content only exists in the DOM (contentEditable div). It is NOT continuously synced to React state or persisted to storage. Any component remount loses everything.

### 2. Idle Conversation Resumption (Most Common)
When returning to a conversation that has been idle, the runtime/sandbox may have been paused or stopped. The system needs to "awaken" the runtime before the WebSocket can connect. If a user submits a message during this startup period, the message is lost.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Leave a conversation idle for a period of time (runtime goes to sleep)
2. Return to the conversation and immediately type and submit a message
3. The input is cleared but the message is lost while the runtime is waking up

### 3. WebSocket Not Yet Connected
When starting a new conversation or returning to an existing one, there is a brief period where the WebSocket connection is being established (status: CONNECTING). If a user submits a message during this window, the message is lost.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Navigate to a conversation
2. Quickly type and submit a message before the connection indicator shows "connected"
3. The input is cleared but the message never appears in the chat

### 4. WebSocket Disconnection During Use
If the WebSocket connection drops (due to network issues, server restart, or other transient failures), messages submitted before reconnection completes are lost.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Be in an active conversation
2. Simulate network disconnection or wait for a server-side disconnect
3. Submit a message before the automatic reconnection completes
4. The input is cleared but the message is not sent

### 5. Page Refresh
If the user refreshes the page (intentionally or due to a browser issue) while typing a message, the draft message is lost.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Start typing a message in the chat input
2. Refresh the page (F5 or browser refresh)
3. The message draft is gone

## Expected Behavior

- **Draft messages should be continuously saved** as the user types (to localStorage or similar)
- Messages typed by the user should never be lost due to background operations
- If a message cannot be sent immediately (runtime waking up, WebSocket connecting), it should be queued and sent when ready
- The input should only be cleared once we have confirmation the message was successfully received

## Current Behavior

- **Typed content only exists in the DOM** - not synced to React state continuously
- The chat input is cleared immediately when the user submits, regardless of whether the message was successfully sent
- There is no queuing mechanism for messages when the runtime is starting or WebSocket is disconnected
- Draft messages are not persisted to browser storage
- Any component remount loses all typed content

## Impact

This issue causes significant user frustration, especially when:
- Writing long, detailed prompts (most painful - can lose minutes of work)
- Returning to conversations after idle periods
- Working on unstable network connections
- Background operations trigger unexpected refreshes
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13280.title: Chat messages can be lost when WebSocket is disconnected or during page refresh

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13327.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug?

- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate.

### Bug Description

OpenHands Version: 1.4.0

Description:

When opening a new conversation without a connected repository, the "Changes" tab in the UI makes a request to GET /api/git/changes/workspace/project. The agent-server's git_router.py passes this relative path to get_git_changes(), which calls get_changes_in_repo() → validate_git_repository(). The path gets concatenated with the container's working directory (/workspace/project), resulting in the duplicated path /workspace/project/workspace/project, which does not exist.

```
Unhandled exception on GET /api/git/changes/workspace/project

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "openhands/agent_server/git_router.py", line 25, in git_changes
  File "openhands/sdk/git/git_changes.py", line 205, in get_git_changes
  File "openhands/sdk/git/git_changes.py", line 54, in get_changes_in_repo
  File "openhands/sdk/git/utils.py", line 206, in validate_git_repository
openhands.sdk.git.exceptions.GitRepositoryError: Directory does not exist: /workspace/project/workspace/project
```

### Expected Behavior

The Changes tab should either show an empty state or gracefully handle the case where no git repository is initialized, instead of returning a 500 error.

### Actual Behavior

_No response_

### Steps to Reproduce

Deploy OpenHands v1.4 with Docker (standard docker compose up)

Open the web UI and click "New conversation" (without connecting a repository)

Open the "Changes" tab on the right panel

Observe the 500 error: "Request failed with status code 500"

### OpenHands Installation Method

CLI (uv tool install)

### If you selected "Other", please specify

_No response_

### OpenHands Version

_No response_

### Version Confirmation

- [ ] I have confirmed this bug exists on the LATEST version of OpenHands

### Model Name

_No response_

### Operating System

None

### Browser (if using web UI)

_No response_

### Logs and Error Messages

OpenHands: 1.4.0 (Docker image docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:1.4)

Agent-server: ghcr.io/openhands/agent-server

OS: Linux (Docker)

Deployment: Behind Nginx reverse proxy with SANDBOX_CONTAINER_URL_PATTERN

### Screenshots and Additional Context

_No response_
