all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13420.body:
### Is there an existing issue for the same bug?

- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate.

### Bug Description

It seems impossible to persist an empty `llm_base_url` from the LLM settings UI, anymore.

Observed behavior:

- In **Basic** view, clicking **Save** can bounce the form back into **Advanced** view with a populated Base URL that the user did not set.
- In **Advanced** view, if the user deletes the Base URL and clicks **Save**, the Base URL comes back.

The settings page cannot truly clear an old/custom Base URL once one existed.

### Expected Behavior

If the user clears the Base URL in Advanced view, or saves from Basic view (which is supposed to clear advanced-only settings), the saved settings should remain empty / unset instead of being repopulated with the old base URL.

### Actual Behavior

An empty `llm_base_url` is rewritten to the old base URL during save.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Settings → LLM Settings**
2. Start from a state where `llm_base_url` was previously set (or where the backend can infer one)
3. In **Advanced** view, delete Base URL and click **Save**
4. Reload the settings page
5. Observe that Base URL is populated again

Also reproducible via Basic view:

1. Switch to **Basic** view
2. Click **Save**
3. Reload the settings page
4. Observe that the form may re-open in **Advanced** mode because `llm_base_url` is non-empty again

### Suspected Cause

This looks like a regression/semantic mismatch introduced around:

- #12766
- #13225

Frontend behavior looks intentional:

- `frontend/src/routes/llm-settings.tsx` basic save explicitly sends `llm_base_url: ""` to clear advanced settings
- advanced save sends the Base URL input value directly, so deleting the field also sends `""`

But the backend then treats that empty string as a signal to auto-detect and repopulate a provider default:

- `openhands/server/routes/settings.py`
  - `if not settings.llm_base_url:`
  - if `None`, preserve existing value for partial updates
  - otherwise, for `""`, compute a default with `LITE_LLM_API_URL` or `get_provider_api_base()`

—-
HUMAN: the agent is confused here a bit: if I had base URL in Settings before, it is the OLD base URL that remains in place, it doesn’t get recomputed, nor cleared. I just cannot get rid of it.
—-

So the current code path conflates:

- `None` = not provided
- `""` = explicitly cleared by the user

That means the UI currently has no way to persist an intentionally empty base URL.

### Why this is user-visible

The frontend decides whether to open in Basic vs Advanced mode partly based on whether `llm_base_url` is non-empty:

- `frontend/src/utils/has-advanced-settings-set.ts`

So once the backend rewrites `""` into a computed URL, the UI can keep re-entering Advanced mode.

### Suggested Fix Direction

Treat `llm_base_url=""` as an explicit clear/unset request instead of auto-detecting a replacement value.

At minimum, the save semantics should distinguish all three states:

- `None`: field omitted / preserve existing value for unrelated partial saves
- `""`: explicitly cleared by user
- non-empty string: explicitly set by user

A regression test should cover both:

1. Basic view save clearing advanced settings
2. Advanced view delete-Base-URL save preserving the empty state after reload

### OpenHands Installation Method

Cloud / hosted app

### OpenHands Version

Current cloud deployment

### Additional Context

This may be related to the earlier fixes around computed base URLs and MCP saves, but it seems to be a separate remaining bug: MCP save preservation (`None`) was fixed, while explicit clearing (`""`) is still impossible.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13420.title: [Bug]: LLM settings cannot persist an empty base URL

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13464.body:
## Bug Description

API keys created in one organization can be used to access endpoints of other organizations the user is a member of. The `api_keys.org_id` field is not validated during authorization - only user membership is checked.

## Expected Behavior

An API key created in Org A should only be usable for Org A endpoints. Using it to access Org B should fail with 403 even if the user is a member of both orgs.

## Actual Behavior

API key validation (`validate_api_key`) returns only `user_id`, ignoring the key's `org_id`. The `require_permission` dependency checks if the user is a member of the target org but does not verify the API key was created for that org.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. User is member of Org A and Org B
2. User creates API key while current_org is Org A (key gets `org_id = A`)
3. User calls `GET /api/orgs/{Org_B_id}/settings` with the key
4. Access is granted (should be denied)

## Relevant Code

- `enterprise/storage/api_key_store.py:validate_api_key()` - returns `user_id` only
- `enterprise/server/auth/authorization.py:require_permission()` - checks membership, not key's org_id

## OpenHands Installation Method

Development workflow

## Additional Context

The `api_keys.org_id` is currently only used for:
- Filtering keys in `list_api_keys()` (shows keys for current org only)
- Audit/association purposes

It is not enforced during authorization, creating a potential security gap where org-scoped keys function as user-scoped identity tokens.
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13464.title: [Bug]: API keys can access cross-org endpoints without org_id validation

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13465.body:
## Bug Description

When authenticating with an API key and calling the `/users/me` (or `/api/user/info`) endpoint, there is no way to determine which organization the API key belongs to.

## Expected Behavior

Since API keys are created with an `org_id` stored in the database, the `/me` endpoint should return this org_id (or the full org info) so callers can identify which org context they are operating in.

## Actual Behavior

The only way to find org information is to call `GET /api/orgs` which returns `current_org_id` - but this is the user's currently active org, not the org the API key was created in. These can differ if the user has switched orgs since creating the key.

To access a specific org's endpoints, you must already know the org_id in advance.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create an API key in Org A
2. Call `GET /api/v1/users/me` with the key
3. Response contains user settings but no org_id
4. Call `GET /api/orgs` - returns `current_org_id` which may differ from the key's org

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Add `org_id` field to the `/users/me` response reflecting the API key's org
2. Add a new endpoint like `GET /api/keys/current` that returns the authenticated key's metadata including `org_id`

## Relevant Code

- `enterprise/storage/api_key.py` - ApiKey model has `org_id` field
- `openhands/app_server/user/user_router.py` - `/users/me` endpoint
- `enterprise/server/routes/user.py` - `/api/user/info` endpoint

## OpenHands Installation Method

Development workflow
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13465.title: [Bug]: /users/me endpoint does not return API key's org_id

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13544.body:
## Is there an existing feature request for this?
- [x] I have searched existing issues and feature requests, and this is not a duplicate.

## Problem or Use Case
As an Org Admin/Owner, the current Settings UX is ambiguous about whether a setting applies to my personal user account or to the entire organization.

Today, the Settings area mixes multiple categories together, so it is not clear whether I am editing:
- Secrets
- API Keys
- MCP
- LLM
- Integrations
- Organization configuration

This creates confusion about the scope of changes and increases the risk of admins making changes under the wrong assumption.

The settings that currently affect the entire organization are:
- Language Model (LLM)
- Organization page (change org name, add credits)
- Organization Members (add/remove members, change role)

Everything else should be clearly presented as Personal Settings.

## Proposed Solution
Make the settings UX explicitly distinguish between **Organization Settings** and **Personal Settings**.

Suggested approach:
- Add clear top-level grouping, tabs, or section headers for **Organization Settings** vs **Personal Settings**
- Add helper text or badges on each settings page indicating the scope, for example:
  - “Applies to your personal account only”
  - “Applies to the entire organization”
- Place these pages under **Organization Settings**:
  - Language Model (LLM)
  - Organization page
  - Organization Members
- Place all remaining settings under **Personal Settings**
- Ensure navigation and page titles reinforce the scope so admins never have to infer it

This should make it obvious, at a glance, whether a change affects only the current user or the full org.

## Alternatives Considered
The current UI can be learned through trial and error or internal knowledge, but that does not scale and is easy to misunderstand, especially for org admins who expect a clear separation of personal vs org-wide controls.

## Priority / Severity
High - Significant impact on productivity

## Estimated Scope
Small - UI tweak, config option, or minor change

## Feature Area
User Interface / UX
Configuration / Settings

## Technical Implementation Ideas (Optional)
Potential implementation options:
- Split the settings IA into two nav groups: Personal and Organization
- Add a small reusable scope banner/badge component to settings pages
- If some pages remain accessible from a shared settings area, include consistent scope copy in the header for every page

## Additional Context
Source feedback from internal Slack discussion:
- “As an Org Admin/Owner, we need to disambiguate the current UX. For example, in settings, it's not clear if I am editing Secrets, API Keys, MCP, LLM, integrations for my own user account within the Org, or at the Org level.”
- “Currently, the settings that affect the entire Org are: Language Model (LLM), Organization page (change Org Name, add credits), Organization Members (add/remove members, change role). Everything else should be Personal Settings.”

Related Slack thread:
https://allhandsai.slack.com/archives/C09SWMK9G9X/p1773954874933719
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13544.title: CLOUD - Organizations - Clarify personal vs organization scope in Settings UX for org admins

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13571.body:
## Problem

Runtime image builds are taking **2-3+ hours** to complete, which significantly impacts PR review and merge velocity. Recent example: https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/actions/runs/23454689336/job/68245621321?pr=13552

This is especially problematic when dependency changes are made or when base image caching is invalidated (e.g., the recent switch to slim image in https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/commit/7fbb499f037e444ad4625f30984d53f192829bee).

## Root Cause Analysis

The job **"Build Runtime Image (nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.12-nodejs22, nikolaik)"** builds a **multi-platform Docker image** for both `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` architectures. The ARM64 build runs under **QEMU emulation** on an x86_64 runner, which is dramatically slower than native builds.

### Time Breakdown by Build Step

| Step | Architecture | Duration | Description |
|------|-------------|----------|-------------|
| #36 | linux/amd64 | ~82 min | Playwright installation + system packages |
| #37 | linux/arm64 | ~86 min | Same step, but emulated |
| #38 | linux/amd64 | ~105 min | User/group setup + chown operations |
| #39 | linux/arm64 | ~104 min | Same step, emulated |

### Why These Steps Are So Slow

1. **Playwright Installation Step**: This step installs system packages (libnss3, libnspr4, libatk-bridge, etc.), installs Playwright with Chromium browser dependencies, and runs `poetry run playwright install --with-deps chromium`. On ARM64 under QEMU:
   - Package installation involves heavy I/O
   - Playwright downloads and extracts large browser binaries (~150+ MB)
   - Everything is being emulated

2. **User Setup Step**: While seemingly simple (creates users/groups and runs `chown`), this step runs `chown -R` on the entire `/openhands` directory which contains all the previously installed Python packages, micromamba environment, and Playwright browsers. Recursively changing ownership of thousands of files under QEMU emulation is very slow.

## Proposed Solutions

### Short-term Improvements

1. **Use native ARM64 runners** - GitHub now offers native ARM64 runners (`ubuntu-24.04-arm`) which would be significantly faster than QEMU emulation
2. **Split builds** - Build AMD64 and ARM64 separately on their native architectures, then combine manifests
3. **Cache more aggressively** - The build appears to not be hitting cache effectively for these steps
4. **Reduce chown scope** - Instead of `chown -R` on large directories, set ownership during file creation

### Medium-term Improvement (Recommended)

**Create a pre-built base image with Playwright included:**

1. Create a separate workflow that builds a `python-nodejs-playwright` base image (infrequently, maybe weekly or on Playwright version bumps)
2. Push it to `ghcr.io/openhands/python-nodejs-playwright:python3.12-nodejs22`
3. Use that pre-built image as the base for runtime image builds

This would eliminate the ~86 minute Playwright installation from every PR build, potentially reducing build time from ~3 hours to ~30-40 minutes.

### Investigation Notes

- `nikolaik/python-nodejs` does not have a Playwright variant
- Microsoft's official Playwright images (`mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v*`) have Node.js but lack Python
- Playwright Python images (`mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/python:v*`) have Python + Playwright but no Node.js
- Building and maintaining our own combined image is likely the best path forward

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Runtime image builds complete in under 1 hour for typical PR changes
- [ ] Multi-platform support (amd64/arm64) is maintained
- [ ] Browser functionality (Playwright) continues to work

## Additional Context

The Dockerfile for the runtime image is dynamically constructed, which adds to the maintenance burden. Long-term, simplifying this architecture would also be beneficial.

/cc @xingyaoww
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13571.title: Reduce runtime image build times (currently 2-3+ hours)

all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13626.body:
## Is there an existing issue for the same bug?

- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate.

## Bug Description

On **OpenHands Cloud (`app.all-hands.dev`)**, **planning mode** fails on my **personal** org (specifically **not** our `alpha-organization-testing` org) with a **LiteLLM authentication error**. I am using `openhands/` as the provider.

I ran into this repeatedly while trying to use planning mode, including runs **with and without an attached image**. I reproduced it at least **3 times** (4 affected conversation IDs listed below).

Important details:
- This happens **specifically in planning mode**.
- In **Secrets / settings / etc. I do not pass a LiteLLM key at any time**.
- I first saw this while using **`minimax2`**.
- I then switched to **`gpt5.2`** and planning mode still failed.
- **`gpt5.2` code mode works**.
- In **`alpha-organization-testing`**, I am using **`gpt5.4`** and it works.

This makes it look like there may be a **planning-mode-specific** issue on Cloud, possibly tied to how the personal org or planning flow resolves provider credentials / model routing.

The visible user-facing error banner shows a LiteLLM auth failure similar to:

```text
litellm.AuthenticationError: AuthenticationError: Litellm_proxyException - Incorrect API key provided: sk-...
```

## Expected Behavior

Planning mode should work on OpenHands Cloud using the selected model/provider, regardless of whether an image is attached.

It should not fail with a LiteLLM authentication error when I have not configured or supplied a LiteLLM key.

## Actual Behavior

Planning mode reaches the UI state around **"Skill Ready" / "Let's work on a plan"** and then fails with a LiteLLM authentication error.

In browser console / network output, I also observed related follow-on failures such as:
- `429` responses for conversation-related endpoints
- repeated WebSocket connection failures to `/sockets/events/...`
- a frontend Monaco diff-editor error after the failure path

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to **`app.all-hands.dev`**
2. Use my **personal org**
3. Start a conversation in **planning mode**
4. Try with **`minimax2`** (also reproduced after switching to **`gpt5.2`**)
5. Try both:
   - with an attached image
   - with **no image attached**
6. Observe that planning mode fails with a LiteLLM authentication error
7. Compare against:
   - **`gpt5.2` code mode**, which works
   - **alpha organization + `gpt5.4`**, which works

## OpenHands Installation Method

OpenHands Cloud (app.all-hands.dev)

## If you selected "Other", please specify

N/A

## OpenHands Version

OpenHands Cloud / latest deployment on `app.all-hands.dev` as observed on 2026-03-27 (exact commit/version unknown)

## Version Confirmation

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the LATEST version of OpenHands

## Model Name

Failing in planning mode:
- `minimax2`
- `gpt5.2`

Working comparisons:
- `gpt5.2` in **code mode** works
- `gpt5.4` in **alpha organization** works

## Operating System

Windows 11

## Browser (if using web UI)

Chrome

## Logs and Error Messages

```text
/api/v1/app-conversations/2bbed11aef494feb90fcd78063a4edec/skills:1  Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 429 ()
/api/conversations/2bbed11aef494feb90fcd78063a4edec/config:1  Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 429 ()
WebSocket connection to 'wss://.../sockets/events/2bbed11aef494feb90fcd78063a4edec?resend_all=true&session_api_key=...' failed
WebSocket connection to 'wss://.../sockets/events/5b623b7f870c477e9c394cc8e6fce444?resend_all=true...' failed
litellm.AuthenticationError: AuthenticationError: Litellm_proxyException - Incorrect API key provided: sk-...
Uncaught Error: TextModel got disposed before DiffEditorWidget model got reset
```

## Screenshots and Additional Context

Affected conversation IDs:
- `fac5f6d053684637b03fdd83fa808df0`
- `4b1b3b60927a4cca96b1c38c606a120f`
- `7a74c4f067674e0f8cca519c87cf2dfe`
- `2bbed11aef494feb90fcd78063a4edec`

Additional context:
- Reproduced multiple times on **personal** org, specifically **not** `alpha-organization-testing`
- Reproduced **with and without image input**
- I do **not** configure a LiteLLM key in secrets/settings
- This appears to be different from normal code-mode behavior because **code mode works** while **planning mode fails**
- The org difference may matter: **`alpha-organization-testing` works**, **personal org fails**

- Screenshots show the error and can be added by the reporter in a follow-up comment if needed
all-hands-ai/OpenHands#13626.title: [Bug]: Planning mode on Cloud fails with LiteLLM authentication error across models provided by OpenHands

heroui-inc/heroui#5738.body: ### HeroUI Version 2.8.4 ### Describe the bug # 🐞 BUG - Autocomplete Generates Duplicate IDs in HeroUI v2.8.4, Breaking ARIA/Accessibility **Summary** When rendering multiple instances of the `Autocomplete` component (from HeroUI), **controls and items are assigned duplicate `id` attributes**. This violates ARIA and HTML accessibility rules, causing issues for screen readers, keyboard navigation, and automated accessibility checkers. --- ### Package Version - **@heroui/react: 2.8.4** (latest as of September 2025; confirmed from [npmjs.com/@heroui/react](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@heroui/react?activeTab=dependents)) --- ### References & Guidelines - **WAI-ARIA & Accessibility Guidelines:** - *Every id attribute value used in ARIA or in labels must be unique in the document. Duplicate IDs cause only the first instance to be recognized by assistive tech, breaking screen reader navigation, focus management, and script logic.* - [Deque axe rule](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.3/duplicate-id-aria) - [W3C ACT Rule](https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/act/rules/3ea0c8/proposed/) - [WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.1 (Parsing)](https://www.sitelint.com/docs/accessibility/code-id-attribute-should-be-unique) - **HeroUI’s Accessibility Principles:** - Built “on React Aria Components for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support.” - [HeroUI Accessibility Statement](https://v3.heroui.com/docs/design-principles) --- ### Your Example Website or App https://github.com/All-Hands-AI/OpenHands/issues/10555 ### Steps to Reproduce the Bug or Issue ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Looking at `alembic upgrade head --sql` shows updating version to be:

```UPDATE alembic_version SET version_num='e1975dedd534' WHERE alembic_version.version_num = '35c5d5057736';```

This is not blocking two transactions to upgrade at the same time. Is there conditional statements in the transaction performing the upgrade that blocks concurrent upgrades to the same version?
